Christchurch Harbour, wild coastal scenery, sand dunes, headland, spit with beach huts. Sights: [Hengistbury Head](http://www.hengistbury-head.co.uk/), Mudeford Spit, Christchurch Harbour. 7 km.

-1.7888672,52.4146286.name: Solihull
-1.7888672,52.4146286.ramblerNote: **[Solihull Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-855)**: Town centre, parks, ornamental gardens, lake, River Blythe, woodland, open fields. Sights: [St. Alphege Church](http://www.solihullparish.org.uk), [Manor House](http://www.themanorhousesolihull.com), [Malvern Park](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), [Parkridge Nature Reserve](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), [Malvern & Brueton Park](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), [Tudor Grange Park](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Taste Collective](http://www.tastecollectivesolihull.co.uk) in Solihull, the [Jamaya](http://www.jamaya.co.uk) in Solihull, the Tap & Tandoor in Solihull, the [Manor House Tea Room](http://www.themanorhousesolihull.com) in Solihull, the Vita in Solihull, or the Parkridge Café in Brueton Park, Solihull. 3 hours.

-1.7998262,52.6868262.name: Lichfield Trent Valley
-1.7998262,52.6868262.ramblerNote: **[Lichfield Trent Valley Circular](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Golf club, heath, woodland, Heart of England Way. Sights: Hopwas Hayes Wood, Whittington Heath. MOD firing range — check access. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/305%20Lichfield.gpx).

-1.804385,52.4384167.name: Olton
-1.804385,52.4384167.ramblerNote: **[Olton to Shirley](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-856)**: Canal towpath, River Cole valley, country park, wetlands, grasslands, woodland, heath, urban paths. Sights: Saxon King on Horse sculpture, [Shire Country Park](https://www.birmingham.gov.uk), [Sarehole Mill Museum](https://www.birminghammuseums.org.uk/sarehole), [Ackers Adventure](https://www.ackers-adventure.co.uk), [Vintage Trains](https://www.vintagetrains.co.uk). Lunch at the [Happy Heart Café](https://www.happyheartcafe.co.uk) in Olton, the Journey's End in Acocks Green, or the [Hungry Hobbit](https://www.facebook.com/hungryhobbitz/) in Hall Green. 3.5 hours.

-1.8064141,51.0705706.name: Salisbury
-1.8064141,51.0705706.publicNote: Combine your pilgrimage to Stonehenge with a beautiful hike.
-1.8064141,51.0705706.privateNote:
-- Rambler-walk issues (auto-generated) --
[salisbury-to-amesbury-via-stonehenge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/salisbury-to-amesbury-via-stonehenge/): The walk ends at 'Amesbury, Library Bus Stop' which is not a mainline railway station — return to Salisbury is by bus (lines Activ8, X4, X5). requiresBus is set to true accordingly.
-- end Rambler-walk issues --
-1.8064141,51.0705706.ramblerNote:
**Salisbury to Mottisfont & Dunbridge**: an alternative variant of the [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/) walk. Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, steep-sided chalk downs, river meadows, Test Valley chalk stream, sculpture park. Sights: Clarendon Palace, [New Art Centre at Roche Court](http://sculpture.uk.com/), [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrews Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Silver Plough](http://silverplough-pitton.co.uk/) in Pitton, or the [Lord Nelson](http://www.the-lord-nelson.co.uk/) in Winterslow. 37 km.

**[Salisbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/salisbury-circular/)**: Historic city, open fields, river valley, pretty villages, chalk downs, ancient drove tracks, steep hillside nature reserve, water meadows. Sights: Salisbury Cathedral, [Old Mill at Harnham](http://oldmillhotelsalisbury.co.uk/), Harnham Slope Nature Reserve, Cranborne Chase Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Strenuous afternoon climbs. Lunch at the [Fox & Goose](http://www.foxandgoose-coombebissett.co.uk/) in Coombe Bissett, the [Coombe Bissett Stores](http://www.coombebissettstores.co.uk/) in Coombe Bissett, the [White Hart](http://www.thewhitehart-bishopstone.co.uk/) in Bishopstone, the [Queen's Head](http://queensheadbroadchalke.co.uk/) in Broad Chalke, or the [Chalke Valley Stores](http://www.chalkevalleystores.co.uk/) in Broad Chalke. 32 km. 7.5 hours.

-1.8172462,53.3648176.name: Edale
-1.8172462,53.3648176.ramblerNote:
**[Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/)**: Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, upland peat bog, scenic ridgeline with panoramic valley views. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Upper Derwent Valley reservoirs, Lose Hill, [Old Nag's Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/). Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/) in Hope. 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edale-circular-via-kinder-scout-and-mam-tor/)**: Rugged moorland plateau, peat bog, gritstone outcrops and boulders, fertile valley floor, steep ridgeline, Dark Peak and White Peak landscapes. Sights: Kinder Scout, Kinder Downfall, Jacob's Ladder, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Great Ridge, Pennine Way. Can be very boggy. Lunch at the [Old Nags Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/) in Edale, or the [Rambler Inn](https://www.dorbiere.co.uk/the-rambler-inn/#) in Edale. 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Kinder Scout Circuit from Edale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kinder-scout-circuit-from-edale/)**: Moorland plateau, boggy ground, gritstone boulder fields, exposed edges, steep cloughs, heather slopes, and high-level ridge paths. Sights: Kinder Scout, Kinder Downfall, Grindslow Knoll, Kinder Low, The Woolpacks, Crowden Tower, Fairbrook Naze, Ringing Roger. Serious navigation hazard. 27 km. 8 hours.

-1.8208401,52.3247544.name: Danzey
-1.8208401,52.3247544.ramblerNote: **[Danzey Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-851)**: Farmland, woodland edge, country lanes, stream crossings, open fields. Sights: Mockley Wood, [St. Mary Magdalene](http://www.tanworthchurch.co.uk), [Umberslade Estate](http://www.umberslade-estate.com), [The Vintage Barn](http://www.facebook.com/thevintagebarnhenley), [Middle England Farm](http://www.middleenglandfarm.co.uk), Avoncroft Museum. Can be muddy. Best in spring. Lunch at the [Bell Inn](http://www.thebellattanworthinarden.co.uk) in Tanworth-in-Arden, or the [Warwickshire Lad](http://www.thewarwickshirelad.co.uk) in Wood End. 1.5 hours.

-1.844423,52.3442757.name: Wood End
-1.844423,52.3442757.ramblerNote: **[Wood End Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-887)**: Warwickshire countryside, river crossing, fields, kissing gates, village roads, railway crossing. Sights: [St. Mary Magdalene](https://www.tanworthchurch.co.uk), [Umberslade Estate](https://www.umberslade-estate.com), River Alne. No shops en route. Lunch at the [Warwickshire Lad](https://www.thewarwickshirelad.co.uk) in Wood End, the [Bell Inn](https://www.thebellattanworthinarden.co.uk) in Tanworth-in-Arden, the [Umberslade's Pizzeria](https://www.umberslade-estate.com) in Umberslade, the [Espresso Farm](https://www.umberslade-estate.com) in Umberslade, or the [Straight From the Cow](https://www.straightfromthecow.co.uk) in Carpenters Hill Farm. 1 hours.

-1.8446544,52.3590972.name: The Lakes
-1.8446544,52.3590972.ramblerNote: **[The Lakes Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-825)**: Reservoirs, lakeside paths, ancient woodland, meadows, heathland remnant, field edges. Sights: [Earlswood Lakes](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Clowes Wood](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), [Earlswood Moathouse](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), [Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre](https://www.facebook.com/earlswoodlakescraftcentre/). Can be muddy. Best in spring or autumn. Lunch at the [Red Lion](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk) in Earlswood, the Reservoir in Earlswood, the [Manor House Tearooms](https://www.facebook.com/earlswoodlakescraftcentre/) in Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre, or the [Limes Country Lodge](https://www.limescountrylodge.co.uk) in Earlswood. 2 hours.

-1.8451962,52.4035742.name: Shirley
-1.8451962,52.4035742.ramblerNote: **[Shirley Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-822)**: Canal towpath, Christmas tree plantations, farmland, forest, lakeside paths. Sights: [Stratford-upon-Avon Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Earlswood Lakes](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre](https://www.facebook.com/earlswoodlakescraftcentre/). Trip hazard: anchored cable. Lunch at the Two Mugs in Shirley, the Frankie's Café in Shirley, the Moreton's Kitchen Bar & Deli in Dickens Heath, the Coffee Cloud in Dickens Heath, the [Bulls Head](https://www.bullsheadearlswood.co.uk) in Earlswood, the [Blue Bell Cider House](https://www.bluebellciderhouse.org) in Earlswood, or the [Reservoir](https://www.hungryhorse.co.uk) in Earlswood. 3 hours.

-1.8514355,52.3919065.name: Whitlocks End
-1.8514355,52.3919065.ramblerNote: **[Whitlocks End to Shirley](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-827)**: Farmland, Christmas tree plantation, canal crossing, suburban pavements and footpaths. Sights: [Akamba Heritage Centre](https://www.akamba.co.uk), [Shirley Park](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/Shirley-Park), Stratford-upon-Avon Canal. Lunch at the [Akamba Heritage Centre](https://www.akamba.co.uk) in Whitlocks End, the [Moreton's Kitchen](https://www.mortonskitchen.co.uk) in Dickens Heath, the [Coffee Cloud](https://www.coffeecloud.co.uk) in Dickens Heath, the [Frankie's Café](https://www.frankiescafeshirley.com) in Shirley, or the [Fiesta del Asado](https://www.fiestadelasado.co.uk) in Shirley. 1 hours.

-1.8614034,52.3665989.name: Earlswood
-1.8614034,52.3665989.privateNote:
-- Rambler-walk issues (auto-generated) --
[earlswood-to-oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/earlswood-to-oxted/): Start "Earlswood" is ambiguous (candidates: (multiple))
-- end Rambler-walk issues --
-1.8614034,52.3665989.ramblerNote: **[Earlswood to Wythall](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-818)**: Farm, heathland, ancient woodland, river crossing, golf course, suburban road. Sights: [River Cole](http://www.birminghamweare.com), [Clowes Wood & New Fallings Coppice](http://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), [Fulford Heath Golf Club](http://www.fulfordheathgolfclub.co.uk), [Transport Museum Wythall](http://www.wythall.org.uk). Can be muddy. Best in spring. Lunch at the [Wythall Fish Bar](http://www.facebook.com/WythallFB) in Wythall, the Select & Save in Wythall, or the [Becketts Farm Shop and Restaurant](http://www.wythall-park.org.uk) in Wythall. 1 hours.

-1.8641943,50.7272094.name: Bournemouth
-1.8641943,50.7272094.privateNote:
-- Rambler-walk issues (auto-generated) --
[branksome-to-swanage-or-corfe-castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/branksome-to-swanage-or-corfe-castle/): The 'To Corfe Castle' distance is listed as '14.5 miles' but no km equivalent is given on the page — this may be approximate. | Both walks end at non-mainline-station points (Swanage is served by the heritage Swanage Railway; Corfe Castle is also on the heritage railway). Return to the mainline requires bus to Wareham or Bournemouth, so requiresBus=true for both.
[wareham-to-swanage-or-kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wareham-to-swanage-or-kingston/): The Purbeck Way East walk ends at Swanage, which is served by the Swanage Steam Railway (heritage) as well as buses (#40 to Wareham, #50 to Bournemouth). The page instructs walkers to return by bus, so requiresBus=true has been set. If Swanage mainline rail connection is restored this may change. | The Purbeck Way South walk ends at Kingston (bus stop for #40 to Wareham) or optionally Worth Matravers (not on bus route, requires walk to B3069). The primary endpoint is Kingston by bus; it is unclear whether Worth Matravers should be treated as a separate variant endpoint.
-- end Rambler-walk issues --
-1.8641943,50.7272094.ramblerNote: **Christchurch to Bournemouth**: a variant of the [Bournemouth to Barton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bournemouth-to-barton-on-sea/) walk. Christchurch Harbour, wild coastal scenery, sand dunes, headland, promenade, cliff-top paths. Sights: [Hengistbury Head](http://www.hengistbury-head.co.uk/), Mudeford Quay, Boscombe Pier. Lunch at the Haven House Inn in Mudeford Quay. 12 km.

-1.8654559,52.3801248.name: Wythall
-1.8654559,52.3801248.ramblerNote: **[Wythall to Shirley](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-830)**: Pasture, meadows, fields, riding school, canal aqueduct, River Cole, suburban streets. Sights: [Transport Museum](http://www.wythall.org.uk), [Wythall Park](http://www.wythall-park.org.uk), [Berry Mound Iron Age Hill Fort](http://www.historicengland.org.uk), Berry Mound Pastures. Broken stile; horses present. Lunch at the [Beckett's Farm](http://www.beckettsfarm.co.uk) in Wythall, the [Drawbridge Inn](http://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk) in Major's Green, or the [Colebrook](http://www.emberinns.co.uk) in Shirley. 1.5 hours.

-1.9131016,53.2607853.name: Buxton
-1.9131016,53.2607853.privateNote:
-- Rambler-walk issues (auto-generated) --
[monsal-trail](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/monsal-trail/): The walk starts at Topley Pike Junction (not a mainline station) and ends at Coombs Road Viaduct (not a mainline station); the nearest stations are Buxton and Matlock, with the Transpeak bus linking them to the walk endpoints. It is unclear whether walkers are expected to take the bus both to the start and from the end, or whether a car-based out-and-back is the primary mode. requiresBus is set true accordingly. | The page describes the walk as 9 miles / 14.5 km but no pure walking time (hours) is given.
-- end Rambler-walk issues --
-1.9131016,53.2607853.ramblerNote:
**[Buxton Circular or to Monsal Dale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-or-to-monsal-dale-via-the-wye-valley/)**: Limestone gorges, stepping stones, overhanging rockfaces, dismantled railway line, limekilns, high pastures, dry valleys, and quiet villages. Sights: Chee Dale, Miller's Dale Viaducts, Five Wells Chambered Tomb, Deep Dale, Wye Valley, Monsal Trail. Paths flood after rain. Lunch at the Angler's Rest in Miller's Dale, or the [Refreshment Room](https://www.blueberryfood.co.uk/the-refreshment-room) in Millers Dale. 26 km. 7.5 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via Axe Edge Moor and Three Shires Head](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-axe-edge-moor-and-three-shires-head/)**: Open upland moor, broad grassy and gravel paths, boggy moorland sections with groughs and peat hags, scenic river valleys, disused quarries, pastures, and long far views. Sights: Solomon's Temple, Three Shires Head, Panniers Bridge, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle). Can be boggy. Lunch at the [Flash Bar Café and Shop](http://www.flashbarstores.co.uk/) in Flash Bar, or the New Inn in Flash. 27 km. 7 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/)**: Quiet moorland plateau, gritstone scarps, green valleys, reservoirs, and hillfort promontory. Sights: Castle Naze, Black Edge, Combs Edge, Combs Reservoir, Corbar Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/) in Combs. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via the Dragon's Back](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-the-dragons-back/)**: Limestone uplands, remote valleys, dramatic reef knoll ridges, dry valleys, disused quarries, and open moorland. Sights: Chrome Hill, Parkhouse Hill, Hitter Hill, High Wheeldon, High Edge, Dowel Dale, Harpur Hill Quarry, Blue Lagoon. Steep scrambling sections. Lunch at the [Packhorse Inn](https://www.thepackhorseinn.net) in Crowdecote, the [Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese](https://www.robinsonsbrewery.com/pubs/ye-olde-cheshire-cheese-inn-longnor) in Longnor, the Longnor Fish and Chip Shop and Tea Room in Longnor, or the Cobbles Coffee Shop in Longnor. 26 km. 7 hours.

**[Buxton to Macclesfield via the Cat and Fiddle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-to-macclesfield/)**: Moorland, exposed ridges, gritstone uplands, wooded valleys, rolling pastures, ancient packhorse routes, and quarried country park. Sights: Shining Tor, Buxton Crescent Hotel, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/the-bar-at-cat-and-fiddle/), Forest Chapel, Macclesfield Forest, Tegg's Nose Country Park. Very exposed moorland. Lunch at the [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk) in Buxton New Road, the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest, or the Stanley Arms Hotel in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest. 24 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Macclesfield to Buxton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-to-buxton/)**: Residential roads, pastures, rolling hills, quarried hilltop, forest tracks, exposed moorland, lonely upland valleys, and riverside approach into a spa town. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Shining Tor, Goytsclough Quarry, Pavilion Gardens, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle/), [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk/). Exposed in bad weather. Lunch at the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest, or the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest. 25 km. 7 hours.

-1.9183726,53.3120572.name: Chapel-en-le-Frith
-1.9183726,53.3120572.ramblerNote: **Buxton to Chapel-en-le-Frith**: a shorter variant of the [Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/) walk. Moorland plateau, gritstone scarps, descent via Castle Naze causeway to valley station. Sights: Castle Naze, Combs Edge, Combs Reservoir. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/) in Combs.

-1.9472858,52.0984969.name: Evesham
-1.9472858,52.0984969.ramblerNote: **[Evesham to Pershore via Dumbleton and Bredon Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/evesham-to-pershore-via-dumbleton-and-bredon-hills/)**: Vale of Evesham market gardens, river meadows, pastures, minor hills, country estate, woods, and Iron Age hillfort summit with panoramic views. Sights: Evesham Abbey Precinct, Dumbleton Hall, Bredon Hill Iron Age Hillfort, Pershore Abbey. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Star Inn](http://thestar-ashtonunderhill.co.uk/) in Ashton-Under-Hill. 34 km. 8 hours.

-1.9924997,52.3609702.name: Barnt Green
-1.9924997,52.3609702.ramblerNote: **[Hagley to Barnt Green via the Clent Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hagley-to-barnt-green-via-the-clent-hills/)**: Ridge walk over North Worcestershire Hills, open hilltops, scrubland, gorse, woods, pastures, motorway bridge crossing, country parks, heathland with bilberry and heather. Sights: [Clent Hills](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clent_Hills), [Wychbury Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wychbury_Hill), [Hagley Park](https://www.hagleypark.co.uk/), The Four Stones, Walton Hill, Waseley Hills Country Park, Beacon Hill Castle, Lickey Hills Country Park, Bilberry Hill, Cofton Hill. Steep descents. 19 km. 5 hours.

-2.0190286,54.9662648.name: Corbridge
-2.0190286,54.9662648.ramblerNote: **Hexham to Corbridge**: a variant of the [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/) walk. River Tyne, country lanes, North Tyne Valley views, Wall stretches, ridge, historic town of Corbridge. Sights: Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, St. Oswald's Church, Halton Chesters Fort, Aydon Castle, Halton Castle, Halton Church. Lunch at the Hadrian Hotel in Wall, or the Errington Coffee House in near Halton Chesters. 21 km.

-2.0230749,51.6767954.name: Kemble
-2.0230749,51.6767954.publicNote: Your gateway to a particularly beautiful part of the Cotswalds. Hike all the way from here to Stroud Station if you're feeling ambitious.
-2.0230749,51.6767954.ramblerNote: **[Kemble Circular via Thames Head and Cirencester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kemble-circular/)**: Cotswolds farmland, disused canal towpath, ancient hillfort, beech woods, high watershed, grand parkland vistas, market town streets, quiet fields, and upper Thames riverbank. Sights: Thames Head, Lyd Well, Trewsbury Camp Hillfort, Thames & Severn Canal, Sapperton Canal Tunnel, Hailey Wood, Cirencester Park, Queen Anne Monument, Roman Amphitheatre, Roman Town Wall, [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Can flood at Thames Head. Lunch at the [Thames Head Inn](https://www.arkells.com/pub/the-thames-head-inn-cirencester.htm) in Kemble, or the [Bell at Sapperton](https://www.bellsapperton.co.uk/) in Sapperton. 27 km. 6 hours.

-2.0549484,50.6382716.name: Corfe Castle
-2.0549484,50.6382716.privateNote:
-- Rambler-walk issues (auto-generated) --
[branksome-to-swanage-or-corfe-castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/branksome-to-swanage-or-corfe-castle/): The 'To Corfe Castle' distance is listed as '14.5 miles' but no km equivalent is given on the page — this may be approximate. | Both walks end at non-mainline-station points (Swanage is served by the heritage Swanage Railway; Corfe Castle is also on the heritage railway). -2.0723991,52.1307399.name: Pershore
-2.0723991,52.1307399.ramblerNote: **[Evesham to Pershore via Dumbleton and Bredon Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/evesham-to-pershore-via-dumbleton-and-bredon-hills/)**: Vale of Evesham market gardens, river meadows, pastures, minor hills, country estate, woods, and Iron Age hillfort summit with panoramic views. Sights: Evesham Abbey Precinct, Dumbleton Hall, Bredon Hill Iron Age Hillfort, Pershore Abbey. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Star Inn](http://thestar-ashtonunderhill.co.uk/) in Ashton-Under-Hill. 34 km. 8 hours.

-2.0787884,51.061005.name: Tisbury
-2.0787884,51.061005.ramblerNote:
**[Tisbury Circular via Alvediston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-alvediston/)**: Chalky downland valleys, open hilltops, coombes, wooded Iron Age hill fort, undulating countryside, expansive views. Plenty of ascent. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloakswallowcliffe.com/) in Swallowcliffe, or the [Horseshoe Inn](http://thehorseshoe-inn.co.uk/) in Ebbesbourne Wake. 26 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Dinton and Fovant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-dinton-and-fovant/)**: Nadder Valley, undulating West Wiltshire Downs, grassy U-shaped valleys, bluebell woods, wildflower meadows, pastures, and chalk downland. Sights: Philipps House, Dinton Park, Dinton Mill, Fovant Badges. Lunch at the Wyndham Arms in Dinton. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Donhead St. Andrew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-donhead-st-andrew/)**: Rolling downland, wooded estates, large country parks, open ridges, Neolithic hill fort, steep descents, and unspoilt village. Sights: [Old Wardour Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/old-wardour-castle/), New Wardour Castle. Can be muddy. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Hindon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-hindon/)**: Dry chalky U-shaped downland valleys, wooded ridges, grassy valleys, picturesque villages, rolling hills, and river valley. Sights: Fonthill Estate, Place Farm, Fonthill House. Lunch at the [Pythouse Kitchen Garden Cafe](http://pythousekitchengarden.co.uk/) in West Hatch, the [Grosvenor Arms](http://www.grosvenorarmshindon.co.uk/) in Hindon, the [Lamb](http://www.lambhindon.co.uk/) in Hindon, or the [Beckford Arms](http://www.beckfordarms.com/) in Fonthill Gifford. 22 km. 5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Ludwell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-ludwell/)**: Upper Nadder Valley, Wardour Park, rolling downland, steep chalk escarpments, ridge walking, woods, boggy brook crossings, and unspoilt village. Sights: New Wardour Castle, Old Wardour Castle, Win Green Hill, White Sheet Hill, Castle Ditches Iron Age Hillfort. Steep rough descents. Lunch at the [Grove Arms](https://www.thegrovearms.co.uk/) in Ludwell, or the [Talbot Inn](http://www.talbotinnberwickstjohn.co.uk/) in Berwick St. John. 26 km. 6.5 hours.

-2.0947934,54.9735335.name: Hexham
-2.0947934,54.9735335.ramblerNote: **Bardon Mill to Hexham**: a variant of the [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/) walk. Common, upland plateau, crags, scenic Wall, gentle descent into North Tyne Valley, riverside, country lanes. Sights: Housesteads Fort, Sycamore Gap, Carrowburgh Fort, Mithraeum, Black Carts Turret, Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, Vindolanda Roman Fort. Lunch at the Housesteads Fort Café in Housesteads, the Chesters Fort Tearoom in Chesters, the Riverside Kitchen Café in Chollerford, or the George Hotel in Chollerford. 28 km.

-2.1219368,53.2593665.ramblerNote:
**[Buxton to Macclesfield via the Cat and Fiddle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-to-macclesfield/)**: Moorland, exposed ridges, gritstone uplands, wooded valleys, rolling pastures, ancient packhorse routes, and quarried country park. Sights: Shining Tor, Buxton Crescent Hotel, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/the-bar-at-cat-and-fiddle/), Forest Chapel, Macclesfield Forest, Tegg's Nose Country Park. Very exposed moorland. Lunch at the [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk) in Buxton New Road, the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest, or the Stanley Arms Hotel in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest. 24 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Macclesfield Circular via Teggs Nose and Kerridge Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-circular-via-teggs-nose-and-kerridge-hill/)**: Steep residential roads, rolling pastures, quarried hilltop, moorland edges, forest, isolated chapel, reservoir, long ridgeline, canal towpath. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Lamaload Reservoir, White Nancy, Kerridge Ridge, Macclesfield Canal. Steep ascents. Lunch at the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/) in Macclesfield Forest, the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, or the Robin Hood in Rainow. 24 km. 7 hours.

**[Macclesfield Circular via The Dane Valley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-circular-via-the-dane-valley/)**: Macclesfield Canal towpath, rolling pastures, wooded valleys, exposed ridge, Dane Valley, tributary valleys, moorland fringes, gritstone ridges, reservoir. Sights: Macclesfield Canal, [Sutton Hall](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/suttonhall/), Croker Hill, Wincle Minn, Greasley Hollow, Dane Valley, Bosley Cloud, Shutlingsloe. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Wincle Beer Company](https://www.winclebeer.co.uk/) in Dane Bridge, Wincle, or the [Ship Inn](https://theshipinnwinclefood.co.uk/) in Wincle. 28 km. 7.5 hours.

**[Macclesfield to Buxton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-to-buxton/)**: Residential roads, pastures, rolling hills, quarried hilltop, forest tracks, exposed moorland, lonely upland valleys, and riverside approach into a spa town. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Shining Tor, Goytsclough Quarry, Pavilion Gardens, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle/), [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk/). Exposed in bad weather. Lunch at the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest, or the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest. 25 km. 7 hours.

-2.1465358,52.4224267.name: Hagley
-2.1465358,52.4224267.ramblerNote: **[Hagley to Barnt Green via the Clent Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hagley-to-barnt-green-via-the-clent-hills/)**: Ridge walk over North Worcestershire Hills, open hilltops, scrubland, gorse, woods, pastures, motorway bridge crossing, country parks, heathland with bilberry and heather. Sights: [Clent Hills](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clent_Hills), [Wychbury Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wychbury_Hill), [Hagley Park](https://www.hagleypark.co.uk/), The Four Stones, Walton Hill, Waseley Hills Country Park, Beacon Hill Castle, Lickey Hills Country Park, Bilberry Hill, Cofton Hill. Steep descents. 19 km. 5 hours.

-2.3181313,52.1093038.name: Great Malvern
-2.3181313,52.1093038.ramblerNote: **[Malvern Hills from Great Malvern or Colwall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/malvern-hills-from-great-malvern-or-colwall/)**: Steep dramatic hills rising from flat vales, ancient woodland, open grassy commons with bracken and heather, scrub and rock ridge, rolling pastures. Sights: Great Malvern Priory, [St. Ann's Well](http://stannswell.co.uk/), Worcestershire Beacon, Herefordshire Beacon, British Camp. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the [Wyche Inn](http://www.thewycheinn.co.uk/) in Malvern, the [Café H2O at the Malvern Hills GeoCentre](https://www.wyche-innovation.com/cafe-h2o) in Malvern, the [Malvern Hills Hotel & Restaurants](http://malvernhillshotel.co.uk/) in Malvern, or the Sally's Place in Malvern. 20 km. 6.5 hours.

-2.3263682,54.3214617.name: Garsdale
-2.3263682,54.3214617.ramblerNote:
**Kirkby Stephen to Garsdale**: a longer variant of the [Nine Standards](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/nine-standards/) walk. High exposed upland watershed, moorland, open fells, source of Eden River, good track above Mallerstang Valley. Sights: Nine Standards, High Seat, Hugh Seat, Hell Gill Bridge. Exposed high ground. 29 km. 8 hours.

**[Wild Boar Fell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wild-boar-fell/)**: Remote moorland ridge, lonely dales, upland fells, scarp edges, river pastures, commons, and bucolic valley farmland. Sights: Pendragon Castle, Wild Boar Fell, Swarth Fell, Mallerstang Edge. Can be boggy. 23 km. 6 hours.

**[Baugh Fell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/baugh-fell/)**: Wild moorland, pathless plateau, rough pasture, lonely valleys, tarns, infant river fords, and a waterfall. Sights: Baugh Fell, Tarn Rigg Hill, Knoutberry Haw, West Baugh Fell Tarn, Grisedale, Ure Force, Dandrymire Viaduct, [Moorcock Inn](https://www.the-moorcock.co.uk/). Pathless, rough ground. 19 km. 6 hours.

-2.3450711,54.9745437.name: Bardon Mill
-2.3450711,54.9745437.ramblerNote: **Haltwhistle to Bardon Mill**: a variant of the [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/) walk. Burn gorge, upland plateau, Roman military zone, crags, Sycamore Gap, lough, open moorland. Sights: Haltwhistle Burn Roman Fortlet, Great Chesters Fort, Sycamore Gap, Crag Lough, Housesteads Fort. Lunch at the Roman Army Museum Tea Room in Walltown Quarry, the Milecastle Inn in near Haltwhistle, the Twice Brewed Inn in near Steel Rigg, the Once Brewed Coffee and Bakehouse at The Sill in near Steel Rigg, or the Housesteads Fort Café in Housesteads. 18 km.

-2.3567991,52.0798427.name: Colwall
-2.3567991,52.0798427.ramblerNote: **Colwall to Great Malvern**: an alternative variant of the [Malvern Hills from Great Malvern or Colwall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/malvern-hills-from-great-malvern-or-colwall/) walk. Fields, oak woods at westerly base of hills, ridge tops, open commons, scrub and bracken slopes. -2.3607303,54.2056394.ramblerNote:
**[Great Knoutberry Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-knoutberry-hill/)**: Grassy moorland, high fells, quiet roads, good tracks, open views, railway viaducts, becks, and pastures. Sights: Dent Head Viaduct, Ribblehead Viaduct, Great Knoutberry Hill, Blea Moor Railway Tunnel, [Station Inn](https://www.thestationinnribblehead.com/), Ribblehead Station Visitor Centre and Tea Room. Can be boggy. 18 km. 5 hours.

**[Ingleborough via Ingleton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-ingleton/)**: High moorland, steep mountain ascent, exposed plateau, limestone pavement, erratics, shake holes, pot holes, farm tracks, wooded gorge option, valley return under viaduct. Sights: Ingleborough, Ribblehead Viaduct, Ribblehead Limestone Quarry National Nature Reserve, Park Fell, Simon Fell, Scales Moor limestone plateau, Ingleton Waterfalls Trail, Ribblehead Station. Very exposed summit. Lunch at the [Wheatsheaf Inn](https://www.thewheatsheaf-ingleton.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the [Peaks & Troughs Bar & Kitchen](https://www.peakstroughs.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the Village Kitchen in Ingleton, the Ingleton Chippy in Ingleton, the Ingleton Pizza in Ingleton, the G&T Café and Kitchen in Ingleton, or the Three Horse Shoes in Ingleton. 24 km. 7 hours.

**[Whernside](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whernside/)**: Moorland, mosses, heather, cotton grass, high plateau with tarns, elevated scarp path, steep descent on engineered path, farm tracks, limestone country with pavements, shake holes, sink holes, and dry streambeds. Sights: Whernside, Ribblehead Viaduct, Artengill Railway Viaduct, Low Force Waterfall. Can be boggy. 20 km. 5.5 hours.

Sights: Nine Standards, Eden River gorge, Ladthwaite Beck waterfall, Dukerdale, Podgill Viaduct. Can be boggy. 18 km. 5 hours.

**[Wild Boar Fell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wild-boar-fell/)**: Remote moorland ridge, lonely dales, upland fells, scarp edges, river pastures, commons, and bucolic valley farmland. Sights: Pendragon Castle, Wild Boar Fell, Swarth Fell, Mallerstang Edge. Can be boggy. 23 km. 6 hours.

Sights: Alfred's Tower, [Hauser & Wirth Somerset](https://www.hauserwirth.com/locations/10068-hauser-wirth-somerset), Bruton Dovecote. Can be muddy. **[Bruton Circular via Stourhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-stourhead/)**: South Somerset pastures, wooded ridge forming the Wiltshire/Somerset boundary, hanging woods, landscaped estate gardens with lakeside walks, grassy U-shaped valley, honey-coloured stone-built town. Sights: Alfred's Tower, Stourhead Estate, Palladian Bridge, Pantheon, Bristol Cross, Stourhead House, Bruton Dovecote, Hauser & Wirth Somerset. Narrow steep-sided valleys and coombes, fast-flowing streams, rolling hilltops, honey-coloured stone villages, far views to Wiltshire and Dorset boundary. Lunch at the [Three Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thethreehorseshoesbatcombe.co.uk/) in Batcombe, the [Matt's Kitchen](http://www.mattskitchen.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [Prickly Pear](https://www.prickly-pear.com/) in Bruton, the [Sun Inn](https://thesuninnbruton.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [At the Chapel](http://www.atthechapel.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [Old Pharmacy](https://www.oldpharmacybruton.com/) in Bruton, the [Briar](https://numberonebruton.com/briar/) in Bruton, the [Blue Ball Bruton](https://www.blueballbruton.com/) in Bruton, the [Da Costa](https://da-costa.co.uk/) in Bruton, or the [Roth Bar](https://rothbar.co.uk/) in Bruton. 29 km.

**Castle Cary to Bruton**: an alternative variant of the [Bruton Circular via Stourhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-stourhead/) walk. South Somerset pastures, wooded ridge, hanging woods, landscaped estate gardens with lakeside walks, grottoes and classical temples. Sights: Alfred's Tower, Stourhead Estate, Palladian Bridge, Pantheon, Stourhead House, Bruton Dovecote, Hauser & Wirth Somerset. Lunch at the [Old Red Lion](http://www.theoldredlion.org.uk/) in North Brewham, the [Spread Eagle Inn](https://www.spreadeagleinn.com/) in Stourton, or the [Stourhead Estate Restaurant](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/stourhead/eating-and-shopping-at-stourhead) in Stourton. 27 km.

Sights: Market House, Cadbury Castle, Lodge Hill, Glastonbury Tor, Mendip Hills, Parrock Hill, The Beacon, Sigwells Plateau. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the Catash Inn in North Cadbury, the Chapel Cross Coffee Rooms in South Cadbury, or the Camelot Inn in South Cadbury. 30 km. 7 hours.

**[Blorenge from Abergavenny](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blorenge/)**: Steep ascent through woodland, tramroad contours, industrial ruins, heather moorland, bracken slopes, glacial bowl, canal towpath, pastures with views. Sights: Abergavenny Castle, Castle Meadows, Llanfoist tunnel, Monmouthshire & Brecon Canal, Llanfoist Inclines, Garnddyrys Forge, Blorenge summit plateau, The Punchbowl, Llanfoist Wharf, Blaenavon World Heritage Site. Steep ascent and descent. 19 km. 6 hours.

**[Sugar Loaf Circular from Abergavenny](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sugar-loaf-abergavenny-circular/)**: Lush pastures, ancient oak woods, heathland, grassy ridges, heather and gorse, open mountain summit, valley stream. Sights: Sugar Loaf, [Sugar Loaf Vineyard Coffee Shop and Tasting Room](https://sugarloafvineyards.co.uk/), Deri Fach oak wood, Mynydd Llanwenarth. Very exposed summit. 19 km. 5.5 hours.

Check tide timings. Best in summer. Lunch at the [Blue Anchor](http://www.blueanchoraberthaw.com) in East Aberthaw. 21 km.

0.0042319,51.2442817.ramblerNote:
**Hurst Green to Oxted**: a shorter variant of the [Hurst Green to Chiddingstone Causeway](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-chiddingstone-causeway/) walk. Greensand hills, woodland, open country, village paths. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Carpenters Arms](http://carpenterslimpsfield.co.uk/) in Limpsfield Chart. 9 km. 3 hours.

**Godstone to Hurst Green**: a shorter variant of the [Godstone to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/godstone-to-oxted/) walk. Low-lying farmland, river valley, woodland, village green, playing fields. Sights: [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), St George's Church Crowhurst, Lagham Manor, Greenwich Meridian Trail. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted) in Staffhurst Wood. 13 km. 3 hours.

**[Hurst Green to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-sevenoaks/)**: Greensand ridge, chart hills, undulating woodland, commons, deer park, historic estates, suburban old town. Sights: Chartwell, Toy's Hill, Ide Hill, Knole Park, Knole Palace. Steep climbs. Lunch at the NT restaurant at Chartwell in Chartwell, or the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxhoundstoyshill.co.uk/) in Toys Hill. 18 km.

**[Hurst Green to Riddlesdown](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-riddlesdown/)**: Heathland common, golf courses, farmland, North Downs escarpment, open downland, woodland strips, and views across the High Weald and South Downs. Sights: Limpsfield Common, River Eden, Titsey church, Tatsfield church, Henley Wood Enclosure, Riddlesdown. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Old Ship in Tatsfield. 21 km. 5.5 hours.

**Hurst Green to Lingfield**: a shorter variant of the [Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/) walk. Hillside paths, Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, old manor houses, flat plain, nature reserve. Sights: [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Tenchleys Manor, St George's Church, Crowhurst Place, Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Community Orchard, Lingfield Oak. Can be muddy. Best in spring. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted) in Staffhurst Wood. 16 km. 4 hours.

Can be muddy. Best in Apr/May or Oct/Nov. Lunch at the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Chingford. 13 km.

0.0089218,51.4657989.ramblerNote: **[Blackheath to Deptford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-deptford/)**: Urban London, genteel residential streets, Georgian and Victorian estates, large open heathland, well-kept parks, river valleys, Victorian cemeteries, and green hilltops with panoramic city views. Sights: Blackheath Cator Estate, Morden College, The Paragon, All Saints' Blackheath, Greenwich Park, St. Margaret of Antioch Church, Boone's Almshouses, Lee Manor House, Manor House Gardens, Mountsfield Park, One Tree Hill, St. Augustine's Church, Camberwell New Cemetery, Honor Oak Crematorium, Rivoli Ballroom, Brockley and Ladywell Cemetery, Hilly Fields Park, Millennium Stone Circle, Blythe Hill Fields, Telegraph Hill Upper Park, Telegraph Hill Lower Park, Deptford Market Yard, Albany Theatre, Ladywell Fields. Lunch at the Mountsfield Park Café in Catford, the Pistachios in the Park in Hilly Fields, or the Blythe Hill Tavern in Honor Oak Park. 22 km. 5 hours.

0.009471,51.6325345.name: Chingford
0.009471,51.6325345.ramblerNote:
**Manor Park to Chingford**: a shorter variant of the [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/) walk. Open flats, commons, woodland, forest rides, and gravel tracks. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Boating Lake in Highams Park, [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre). Can be muddy. Lunch at the Blakesley Arms in Manor Park, the Lakeside Diner in Whipps Cross, the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/) in Highams Park, or the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Rangers Road. 12 km.

**Leytonstone to Chingford**: a shorter variant of the [Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/) walk. Open woodland, earth tracks, grassy paths, ponds, gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge), Epping Forest. Can be muddy. Best in Apr/May or Oct/Nov. Lunch at the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Chingford. 9 km.

0.0095073,51.3765959.ramblerNote:
**[Hayes to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hayes-to-knockholt/)**: Fields, woods, valleys, heathland, commons, and London Green Belt countryside. Sights: [Down House](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/server.php?show=nav.14922), Keston Ponds, Caesar's Well, Wilberforce Oak, Holwood House, Downe Bank Nature Reserve, St Peter and St Paul Church, Hayes Common. Can be waterlogged. Lunch at the [Blacksmith's Arms](http://www.theblacksmithsarms.co.uk/) in Cudham, the Queens Head in Downe, or the George & Dragon in Downe. 16 km.

**[Whyteleafe to Hayes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-to-hayes/)**: Gently rolling countryside, shady footpaths and bridleways, open valleys, woods, chalk downland, and quiet rural hamlets near the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Biggin Hill Airport](https://www.military-airshows.co.uk/ven10.htm), Hutchinson's Bank Local Nature Reserve, Greatpark Clock Tower, Coal Tax posts, Hayes Common, London Outer Orbital Path, Leaves Green Circular Walk, Nash Circular Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [White Bear](https://www.whitebearwarlingham.co.uk) in Fickleshole, or the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham) in Great Farleigh Green. 16 km. 4 hours.

0.0113494,50.8705975.name: Lewes
0.0113494,50.8705975.ramblerNote:
**[Hassocks to Lewes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-lewes/)**: South Downs chalk grassland ridge, panoramic views to sea and inland, dew ponds, Iron Age fort, wooded climbs, riverside embankment, historic town. Sights: Church of St John the Baptist, [Jill Windmill](http://www.jillwindmill.org.uk), Jack Windmill, Ditchling Beacon, [Chattri Memorial](http://www.chattri.com/), [Old Hamsey Church](http://www.friendsofhamsey.org/history/), Anne of Cleves House, Lewes Castle, Barbican House Museum, Lewes Priory, St John Sub Castro, Bull House. Can be slippery. Lunch at the Half Moon in Plumpton. 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/)**: Rambler favourite! Three distinct downland ridges, wide open chalk paths, panoramic views, historic town streets, gentle valleys. Sights: Mount Caburn, Lewes. Three steep climbs. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, the YHA Courtyard Cafe in Southease, the Trevor Arms in Glynde, the Steamworks in Glynde, or the Little Cottage Tea Rooms in Glynde. 23 km.

**[Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/)**: Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, woods, wetland, bridleways, old racecourse, and town streets. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Lewes Old Racecourse, Lewes Castle, Barbican House Museum, Black Cap, Battle of Lewes viewpoint, Baxter's Field, Ashcombe Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Half Moon in Plumpton, or the Swan Inn in Falmer. 23 km.

**Lewes to Southease**: a shorter variant of the [Lewes to Saltdean](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-saltdean/) walk. Chalk grassland ridges, South Downs views, historic town, picturesque village, return by train from Southease. Sights: Lewes Castle, Lewes Priory, Anne of Cleves House, Monk's House. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 12 km.

**[Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/)**: South Downs ridges, open downland, sweeping valley, remote hidden valley, coastal shingle beach, recreation ground. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort, Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Lunch at the Ram Inn in West Firle. 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Lewes via West Firle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-via-west-firle-circular/)**: Steep hills, chalk ridge, South Downs Way, secluded valley, flat river levee, historic town. Sights: Lewes Castle, Mount Caburn, Firle Place, Monk's House, Harveys Brewery, Parish Church of St Thomas a Becket, White Hart Hotel. Steep hills. Avoid Dec/Jan. Lunch at the Ram Inn in Firle. 23 km. 6 hours.

**Lewes to Brighton**: a variant of the [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/) walk. Chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, seafront promenade, urban Brighton. Sights: Lewes Castle, Kipling Gardens, Brighton Pier, [Brighton Royal Pavilion](http://www.royalpavilion.org.uk/). Lunch at the Juggs in Kingston. 19 km.

**Lewes to Seaford**: a longer variant of the [Southease to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-seaford/) walk. River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland with coastal views, pretty villages, seaside esplanade. Sights: Monk's House, St Andrew's Church. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 24 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Uckfield to Lewes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-lewes/)**: River valleys, fields, woods, golf course, open chalk downland, South Downs escarpment, historic town. Sights: Horsted Place, Lavender Line (Dingley Dell terminal), Glyndebourne opera house, Glyndebourne Wind Turbine, Southeram Nature Reserve, Lewes Castle, Caburn Hillfort. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Anchor Inn in Barcombe Mills. 20 km.

**Bishopstone to Southease**: a shorter variant of the [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/) walk. Downland climbs, South Downs Way ridge walking, descent to Southease. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. 16 km.

**Lewes to Southease**: a shorter variant of the [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/) walk. Rambler favourite! Downland ridge, long gradual descent to Rodmell, chalk paths, open views. Sights: Mount Caburn. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, or the YHA Courtyard Cafe in Southease. 11 km.

**Lewes to Southease**: a shorter variant of the [Lewes to Saltdean](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-saltdean/) walk. Chalk grassland ridges, South Downs views, historic town, picturesque village, return by train from Southease. Sights: Lewes Castle, Lewes Priory, Anne of Cleves House, Monk's House. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 12 km.

**Glynde to Southease**: a shorter variant of the [Lewes via West Firle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-via-west-firle-circular/) walk. South Downs Way chalk ridge, extensive views inland and to Newhaven and the Channel. Sights: Firle Place. Lunch at the Ram Inn in Firle. 11 km.

**[Southease to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/)**: Open treeless downland ridges, steep ascents, scenic Cuckmere Valley, coastal promenade, beach, and pier. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, Beachy Head, Wish Tower, Eastbourne Pier, Italian Gardens. Steep ascents. Lunch at the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop in Alfriston. 27 km.

**[Southease Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-circular/)**: South Downs hills, chalk paths, river embankment, coastal clifftop, Undercliff sea-level promenade, open downland, arable fields, and quiet downland valleys. Sights: Centenary Park, Meridian Monument, King George V Memorial, Undercliff Walk, Telscombe Tye, St Peter's Church Southease, Piddinghoe Church, Harvey's Cross, Breaky Bottom Vineyard, Rottingdean Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Papachino's Gateway Cafe in Peacehaven, the Dewdrop Inn in Peacehaven, the [Burger and Bird](https://www.burgerandbird.com/) in Peacehaven, the Big Mouths Burger Bar in Peacehaven, the [Stonehouse Pizza and Carvery](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven) in Peacehaven, the [Maloncho](https://www.maloncho-restaurant.co.uk/) in Peacehaven, the [Kappadokya](https://www.kappadokya.co.uk/) in Telscombe Cliffs, the Thai Elephant Two in Telscombe Cliffs, the Tavern at Telscombe Cliffs in Telscombe Cliffs, or the [Smugglers' Rest](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thesmugglersrestpeacehaven#/) in Telscombe Cliffs. 23 km.

**Southease circular**: a shorter variant of the [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/) walk. Flat Ouse valley, riverbank, chalk downs, South Downs Way, circular route back to station. Sights: Monk's House, Lewes Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, or the Juggs in Kingston. 15 km.

**[Southease to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-seaford/)**: River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland with coastal views, pretty villages, seaside esplanade. Sights: Monk's House, St Andrew's Church. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

0.0365138,51.7754392.name: Roydon
0.0365138,51.7754392.ramblerNote: **[Roydon to Sawbridgeworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/roydon-to-sawbridgeworth/)**: Gently rolling Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, waterways, green lanes, quiet country roads, river stretches, and navigation canal. Sights: [Henry Moore Foundation](https://henry-moore.org/studios-and-gardens/). Cattle in pastures. Avoid end Oct–Easter. Lunch at the Henry Moore Foundation Visitor Café in Perry Green. 22 km. 5 hours.

0.0450354,51.5521996.name: Manor Park
0.0450354,51.5521996.ramblerNote: **[Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, open flats, commons, forest rides, gravel tracks, residential roads, and meadows. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort, Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Connaught Water, Boating Lake in Highams Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Blakesley Arms in Manor Park, the Lakeside Diner in Whipps Cross, the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/) in Highams Park, the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Rangers Road, the Original Tea Hut in High Beach, the Oyster Shack & Seafood Bar in High Beach, the King's Oak Café in High Beach, the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home) in High Beach, the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/) in Ivy Chimneys, the [Perky Blenders](https://perkyblenders.com/) in Ivy Chimneys, the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//) in Epping, the Dada Café in Epping, the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/) in Epping, the Black Lion in Epping, or the [Funky Monk](https://funkymonk.co.uk/) in Epping. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

0.0468161,51.6259439.ramblerNote: **Manor Park to Buckhurst Hill**: a shorter variant of the [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/) walk. Open flats, commons, woodland, forest rides, and gravel tracks. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Boating Lake in Highams Park, Connaught Water. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Blakesley Arms in Manor Park, the Lakeside Diner in Whipps Cross, the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/) in Highams Park, the [Three Colts](http://thethreecolts.com/) in Buckhurst Hill, or the Railway Bar & Grill in Buckhurst Hill. 14 km.

**[Newhaven Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-circular/)**: Clifftop coast path, woods and fields, river bank, South Downs foothills, hills, shingle beach, and deserted village ruins. Sights: Newhaven Fort, St John the Evangelist Church, Tide Mills, [Newhaven and Seaford Sailing Club](http://sailinginsussex.org/galley), Bishopstone Beach Halt station platform, Chaley Heritage Marine Hospital excavations. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Bridge Inn in Newhaven, the Ark in Newhaven, the Mamoosh Riverside in Newhaven, the Abyss Beer and Pizza in Newhaven, the [Flying Fish](https://www.flyingfishdenton.co.uk/) in Denton, Newhaven, or the Hampden Arms in South Heighton, Newhaven. 24 km.

**[Newhaven to Brighton and Portslade](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-to-brighton-and-portslade/)**: Harbourside path, white chalk cliffs, clifftop and undercliff concrete paths, shingle beach, busy seaside promenade and boardwalk. Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, Saltdean Lido. Exposed in high wind. Lunch at the Hope Inn in Newhaven, the [Peacehaven](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven) in Peacehaven, the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern) in Telescombe, the Smuggler's Rest in Peacehaven, the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/) in Saltdean, the Molly's at the Beach in Rottingdean, the White Horse in Rottingdean, or the West Quay in Brighton Marina. 18 km.

Sights: Newhaven Fort, St John the Evangelist Church, Tide Mills, [Newhaven and Seaford Sailing Club](http://sailinginsussex.org/galley), Bishopstone Beach Halt station platform, Chaley Heritage Marine Hospital excavations. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Bridge Inn in Newhaven, the Ark in Newhaven, the Mamoosh Riverside in Newhaven, the Abyss Beer and Pizza in Newhaven, the [Flying Fish](https://www.flyingfishdenton.co.uk/) in Denton, Newhaven, or the Hampden Arms in South Heighton, Newhaven. 24 km.

**[Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/)**: Open woodland, densely forested areas, grassy paths, earth tracks, ponds, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge), Epping Forest, High Stone. Can be muddy. Best in Apr/May or Oct/Nov. Lunch at the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Chingford. 13 km.

**[Loughton to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/loughton-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, pollarded oaks, beeches and hornbeams, open countryside, farmland, parkland near a ruined country house, and bustling market town. Sights: Epping Forest, Loughton Camp, [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre), [Copped Hall](http://www.coppedhalltrust.org.uk/). Can be muddy. Best in winter/early spring. Lunch at the [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre) in High Beach, the [Kings Oak](http://www.kingsoakhotel.com/) in High Beach, or the [Horseshoes](https://www.instagram.com/thehorseshoesupshire/?hl=en) in Upshire. 13 km. 4 hours.

0.0703481,50.8591176.name: Glynde
0.0703481,50.8591176.privateNote: LON Trainline says: 1h25m — Victoria via Lewes
0.0703481,50.8591176.ramblerNote:
**Bishopstone to Glynde**: a shorter variant of the [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/) walk. Downland climbs, South Downs Way ridge walking westward, descent to Glynde. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. 16 km.

**[Glynde to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/glynde-to-seaford/)**: Rambler favourite! South Downs ridge, chalk downland, Firle Park, river valley, ancient villages, forest, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and esplanade. Sights: Firle Place, Church of St Peter, Alfriston Clergy House, Parish Church of St Andrew, Seaford Museum. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the George Inn in Alfriston, the Star Inn in Alfriston, the Ye Olde Smugglers Inn in Alfriston, the Wingrove Inn in Alfriston, the Chestnuts Tea Room in Alfriston, the Singing Kettle in Alfriston, the Poco Restaurant & Wine Bar in Alfriston, the Plough and Harrow pub in Litlington, or the Littlington Tea Gardens in Litlington. 23 km. 7 hours.

**Glynde to Southease**: a shorter variant of the [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/) walk. Rambler favourite! Downland ridge, South Downs Way, open chalk paths, panoramic views. Lunch at the YHA Courtyard Cafe in Southease. 6 km.

**Glynde to Seaford**: a shorter variant of the [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/) walk. South Downs ridges, open downland, hidden valley, coastal approach. Sights: Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Best in spring/autumn. Lunch at the Ram Inn in West Firle. 16 km.

**Lewes to Glynde**: a shorter variant of the [Lewes via West Firle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-via-west-firle-circular/) walk. Steep hill with views, secluded valley, chalk downland. Sights: Lewes Castle, Mount Caburn. Steep hill. 5 km.

**Glynde to Eastbourne**: an alternative variant of the [Southease to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/) walk. Open treeless downland ridges, steep ascents, scenic Cuckmere Valley, coastal promenade, beach, and pier. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, Beachy Head, Wish Tower, Eastbourne Pier, Italian Gardens. Steep ascents. Lunch at the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop in Alfriston. 27 km.

0.0745614,51.388626.name: Petts Wood
0.0745614,51.388626.ramblerNote: **[Petts Wood Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petts-wood-circular/)**: Suburban woodlands, wide woodland tracks, heathland, valleys, moated manor ruins, open green spaces. Sights: [Scadbury Park](https://www.scadbury-park.org.uk/), [Chislehurst Caves](http://chislehurst-caves.co.uk/), William Willett Memorial Sundial, Scadbury Park Moated Manor House Ruins, St Nicholas Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadhotel.co.uk/) in Chislehurst. 12 km.

0.0826201,50.7799859.name: Bishopstone
0.0826201,50.7799859.ramblerNote:
**[Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/)**: Steep repeated climbs and descents, chalk downland, grassy ridges, vineyard tracks, valley paths, quiet village lanes, open hilltop views to sea and Downs. Sights: Saxon Church of St Andrew, Long Burgh Barrow, St Michael and All Angels church, [Cuckmere Pilgrim Path](http://cuckmerepilgrimpath.org.uk), Bishopstone station pillbox, Alciston Court Farm dovecote. Can be overgrown. Best in spring or autumn. Lunch at the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/) in Berwick. 21 km.

**Southease to Bishopstone**: a shorter variant of the [Southease to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-seaford/) walk. River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland, pretty village. Sights: Monk's House, St Andrew's Church. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 16 km.

**Bishopstone to Seaford**: an alternative variant of the [Seaford Circular via Alfriston and Bishopstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-circular/) walk. Seafront, chalk downland, vineyard tracks, open ridgelines, and coastal cliff finish over Seaford Head. Sights: Seaford Head, Rathfinny Vineyard, Alfriston Church, George Inn, Bishopstone Church. Steep coastal climbs. Lunch at the George Inn in Alfriston. 22 km.

0.0948105,51.1813846.name: Hever
0.0948105,51.1813846.ramblerNote:
**[Cowden to Hever](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-hever/)**: Remote wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, undulating High Weald farmland, vineyard, Eden Valley meadows, quaint village streets, historic castle grounds. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, St Mary, Chiddingstone, St Peter, Hever, Chiding Stone, Leicester Square, Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Edenbridge Town to Hever**: a longer variant of the [Edenbridge Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/) walk. Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, flat Eden Valley, fields to Hever. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch at the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com) in Edenbridge, or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk) in Edenbridge. 18 km. 4 hours.

**[Hever to Ashurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-ashurst/)**: Low hills, wooded valleys, farm fields, bluebell woods, manor house grounds, golf course, orchards, and remote High Weald countryside. Sights: Crippenden Manor, Leighton Manor, Waystrode Manor, Sussex Border Path, Sussex House Farm, Heathersome's Wood, Bolebroke Castle, [Orchard Tea Room](https://www.perryhillorchards.co.uk/orchard-tea-room), St Mary Magdalene, [Cowden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cowden). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaincowden.co.uk) in Cowden. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Hever to Dormans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-dormans/)**: Undulating countryside, woods, farmland, fields, streams, historic manor houses, iron-age hillfort, remote border area of Kent, Surrey and Sussex. Sights: Cowden, St Mary Magdalene Church, Sussex Border Path, Waystrode Manor, Crippenden Manor, Vanguard Way, Dry Hill Fort, Greathed Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaininncowden.com/) in Cowden. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Hever to Leigh](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-leigh/)**: Undulating hills, open fields, beech woods, rivers, ponds, quaint villages, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), St Peter's Church, Chiddingstone Castle, St Mary's Church, Chiding Stone, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), St John the Baptist Church, Luke Tapestry. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/) in Chiddingstone, the Tulip Tree Tearooms in Chiddingstone, or the [Leicester Arms Hotel](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/) in Penshurst. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/)**: Gentle farmland, orchards, river valleys, parkland, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, Withers, Straight Mile, Powder Mills. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst. 16 km. 3.5 hours.

0.0966299,50.9687382.name: Uckfield
0.0966299,50.9687382.ramblerNote:
**[Robertsbridge to Uckfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/robertsbridge-to-uckfield/)**: Linear walk across East Sussex, farmland, fields, villages, and countryside between Robertsbridge and Uckfield. Can be overgrown. Lunch at the [Swan Inn](https://swaninndallington.co.uk/) in Wood's Corner, Heathfield, the [Half Moon Inn](https://www.halfmoon-inn.co.uk/) in Cade Street, Heathfield, the [Star Inn](https://www.starinnoldheathfield.co.uk/) in Old Heathfield, Heathfield, the [Runt In Tun](https://www.runtintun.co.uk/) in Heathfield, the [Star Inn](https://www.thestarinnwaldron.co.uk/) in Waldron, Heathfield, or the [Blackboys Inn](https://www.theblackboys.co.uk/) in Blackboys, Uckfield. 38 km.

**[Uckfield to Buxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-buxted/)**: Undulating Low Weald countryside, Uck Valley, golf course, stream valleys, old deer park, parkland, and bluebell woods. Sights: East Sussex National Golf Course, High Cross Estate, Tickerage Mill, Buxted Park, Buxted House. Can be muddy. Best in bluebell season. Lunch at the [Blackboys Inn](http://www.theblackboys.co.uk/) in Blackboys. 21 km. 5 hours.

**[Uckfield to Lewes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-lewes/)**: River valleys, fields, woods, golf course, open chalk downland, South Downs escarpment, historic town. Sights: Horsted Place, Lavender Line (Dingley Dell terminal), Glyndebourne opera house, Glyndebourne Wind Turbine, Southeram Nature Reserve, Lewes Castle, Caburn Hillfort. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Anchor Inn in Barcombe Mills. 20 km.

**Uckfield to Balcombe**: a shorter variant of the [Uckfield to Pease Pottage](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-pease-pottage/) walk. Linear walk through East Sussex countryside, villages, and farmland ending at Balcombe station. Lunch at the [Griffin Inn](https://thegriffininn.co.uk/) in Fletching, the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownhorstedkeynes.co.uk/) in Horsted Keynes, or the [Green Man](http://greenmanhorstedkeynes.co.uk/) in Horsted Keynes. 29 km.

**[Uckfield via Van Hoogstraaten's Path Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-via-van-hoogstraatens-path-circular/)**: Undulating low Weald countryside, fields, green lanes, woods, golf course, and suburban estates. Sights: [Hempstead Nature Reserve](http://www.localauthoritypublishing.co.uk/councils/uckfield/enviro.html), [Sussex Horse Rescue Centre](http://sussexhorserescuetrust.org.uk/), [Framfield](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Framfield), [East Sussex National Golf Resort and Spa](http://www.eastsussexnational.co.uk/dine-drink/hotel-lounge-bar/), [Boothland Wood](http://www.localauthoritypublishing.co.uk/councils/uckfield/enviro.html), [Uckfield](http://www.localauthoritypublishing.co.uk/councils/uckfield/index.html), Framfield Church. Quite a few stiles. Lunch at the Hare and Hounds in Framfield, or the [Half Way House Tea Room](http://www.eastsussexnational.co.uk/dine-drink/hotel-lounge-bar/) in East Sussex National Golf Resort. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

0.1000064,50.7729203.name: Seaford
0.1000064,50.7729203.privateNote:
LON Trainline says 1h25m - Victoria via Lewes

-- Rambler-walk issues (auto-generated) --
[berwick-to-birling-gap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-birling-gap/): All walk variants end at non-station points (Birling Gap, Friston, East Dean, Exceat) requiring bus to Eastbourne or Seaford — none are station-to-station walks. The page does not give pure walking hours for any variant.
