0.1000064,50.7729203.ramblerNote:
**[Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/)**: South Downs chalk hills, open downland, river valley, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and picturesque village. Sights: Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, High and Over Hill, Berwick church, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Long Man Inn in Wilmington, the George Inn in Alfriston, the Cuckmere Inn in Exceat, the Trawlers in Seaford, the Plough Inn in Seaford, or the Steamworks in Seaford. 20 km.

**Bishopstone to Seaford**: a longer variant of the [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/) walk. All main walk terrain plus seafront promenade into Seaford. Sights: Saxon Church of St Andrew, Long Burgh Barrow, St Michael and All Angels church. Can be overgrown. Lunch at the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/) in Berwick. 23 km.

**[Glynde to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/glynde-to-seaford/)**: Rambler favourite! South Downs ridge, chalk downland, Firle Park, river valley, ancient villages, forest, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and esplanade. Sights: Firle Place, Church of St Peter, Alfriston Clergy House, Parish Church of St Andrew, Seaford Museum. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the George Inn in Alfriston, the Star Inn in Alfriston, the Ye Olde Smugglers Inn in Alfriston, the Wingrove Inn in Alfriston, the Chestnuts Tea Room in Alfriston, the Singing Kettle in Alfriston, the Poco Restaurant & Wine Bar in Alfriston, the Plough and Harrow pub in Litlington, or the Littlington Tea Gardens in Litlington. 23 km. 7 hours.

**Lewes to Seaford**: a longer variant of the [Lewes to Saltdean](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-saltdean/) walk. Chalk grassland ridges, South Downs, Southease station midpoint, extended coastal finish at Seaford. Sights: Lewes Castle, Monk's House. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 24 km.

**[Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/)**: South Downs ridges, open downland, sweeping valley, remote hidden valley, coastal shingle beach, recreation ground. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort, Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Lunch at the Ram Inn in West Firle. 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Seaford to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-to-eastbourne/)**: Rambler favourite! Dramatic clifftop chalk downs, undulating Seven Sisters cliffs, shingle beaches, river haven, elegant seafront promenade. Sights: [Birling Gap](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/birling-gap-and-the-seven-sisters), Seven Sisters, Belle Tout, Martello Tower. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the Cuckmere Inn in Exceat, the Saltmarsh Kitchen in Exceat, or the Grab and Go in Exceat. 21 km.

**[Southease to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-seaford/)**: River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland with coastal views, pretty villages, seaside esplanade. Sights: Monk's House, St Andrew's Church. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Seaford Circular via Alfriston and Bishopstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-circular/)**: Steep coastal cliffs, chalk downland, open panoramic ridgelines, vineyard tracks, village greens, shingle beach, and seafront promenade. Sights: Seaford Head, Martello Tower, Hope Gap, Rathfinny Vineyard, Alfriston Church, George Inn, South Hill Barn, Bishopstone Church. Steep coastal climbs. Lunch at the George Inn in Alfriston, the Brewster's Coffee Co in South Hill Barn, or the Papuchinos at the Galley in Seaford. 25 km.

0.1033213,51.6717759.name: Theydon Bois
0.1033213,51.6717759.ramblerNote: **[Theydon Bois to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/theydon-bois-to-epping/)**: Marshland, woods, gently rolling meadow-land, scenic hamlets, quiet tarmac roads with grassy verges, motorway crossings. Sights: GreenAcres, [Theydon Oak pub](https://theydonoak.co.uk/). Boardwalks rotten in places. Lunch at the [Dog House](https://www.facebook.com/themoletrappubepping/) in Woodhatch, the [Kings Head](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thekingsheadnorthweald#/) in North Weald, or the [Garnon Bushes](https://www.garnonbushespub.com/) in Theydon Garnon. 15 km. 4.5 hours.

0.1098981,51.1556295.name: Cowden
0.1098981,51.1556295.ramblerNote:
**[Cowden to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-eridge/)**: Weald pasture, woods, hills, undulating fields, and valley paths. Sights: Pooh Corner, Old Buckhurst, Church of St Michael and All Angels. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com/) in Hartfield, or the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk/) in Withyham. 21 km. 6 hours.

**[Cowden to Hever](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-hever/)**: Remote wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, undulating High Weald farmland, vineyard, Eden Valley meadows, quaint village streets, historic castle grounds. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, St Mary, Chiddingstone, St Peter, Hever, Chiding Stone, Leicester Square, Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Cowden to Ashurst**: a shorter variant of the [Hever to Ashurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-ashurst/) walk. Low hills, wooded valleys, farm fields, bluebell woods, manor house grounds, golf course, orchards, and remote High Weald countryside. Sights: Waystrode Manor, Sussex Border Path, Sussex House Farm, Heathersome's Wood, Bolebroke Castle, [Orchard Tea Room](https://www.perryhillorchards.co.uk/orchard-tea-room), St Mary Magdalene, [Cowden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cowden). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaincowden.co.uk) in Cowden. 12 km. 3 hours.

**Cowden to Dormans**: a shorter variant of the [Hever to Dormans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-dormans/) walk. Undulating countryside, woods, farmland, fields, streams, historic manor houses, iron-age hillfort, remote border area of Kent, Surrey and Sussex. Sights: Cowden, St Mary Magdalene Church, Sussex Border Path, Waystrode Manor, Crippenden Manor, Vanguard Way, Dry Hill Fort, Greathed Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaininncowden.com/) in Cowden. 13 km.

0.1139888,51.6936318.name: Epping
0.1139888,51.6936318.ramblerNote:
**[Epping to Ingatestone via Chipping Ongar](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/epping-to-ingatestone-via-chipping-ongar/)**: Mildly undulating Essex countryside, ancient woods, arable fields, historic villages, old churches, manor houses, London Clay subsoil. Sights: Chipping Ongar Motte-and-Double-Bailey, St. Andrew's Church, Greensted-juxta-Ongar, St. Laurence Church, Blackmore, Gernon Bushes SSSI. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Theydon Oak in Coopersale. 26 km. 6 hours.

**[Theydon Bois to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/theydon-bois-to-epping/)**: Marshland, woods, gently rolling meadow-land, scenic hamlets, quiet tarmac roads with grassy verges, motorway crossings. Sights: GreenAcres, [Theydon Oak pub](https://theydonoak.co.uk/). Boardwalks rotten in places. Lunch at the [Dog House](https://www.facebook.com/themoletrappubepping/) in Woodhatch, the [Kings Head](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thekingsheadnorthweald#/) in North Weald, or the [Garnon Bushes](https://www.garnonbushespub.com/) in Theydon Garnon. 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, open flats, commons, forest rides, gravel tracks, residential roads, and meadows. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort, Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Connaught Water, Boating Lake in Highams Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Blakesley Arms in Manor Park, the Lakeside Diner in Whipps Cross, the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/) in Highams Park, the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Rangers Road, the Original Tea Hut in High Beach, the Oyster Shack & Seafood Bar in High Beach, the King's Oak Café in High Beach, the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home) in High Beach, the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/) in Ivy Chimneys, the [Perky Blenders](https://perkyblenders.com/) in Ivy Chimneys, the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//) in Epping, the Dada Café in Epping, the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/) in Epping, the Black Lion in Epping, or the [Funky Monk](https://funkymonk.co.uk/) in Epping. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**Leytonstone to Epping**: a longer variant of the [Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/) walk. Open woodland, earth tracks, forest trails, ponds, Iron Age earthworks, gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge), Epping Forest, Loughton Camp, Ambresbury Banks. Can be muddy. Best in Oct/Nov. Lunch at the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Chingford. 20 km.

**[Loughton to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/loughton-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, pollarded oaks, beeches and hornbeams, open countryside, farmland, parkland near a ruined country house, and bustling market town. Sights: Epping Forest, Loughton Camp, [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre), [Copped Hall](http://www.coppedhalltrust.org.uk/). Can be muddy. Best in winter/early spring. Lunch at the [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre) in High Beach, the [Kings Oak](http://www.kingsoakhotel.com/) in High Beach, or the [Horseshoes](https://www.instagram.com/thehorseshoesupshire/?hl=en) in Upshire. 13 km. 4 hours.

0.1308295,51.3457689.name: Knockholt
0.1308295,51.3457689.ramblerNote:
**[Hayes to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hayes-to-knockholt/)**: Fields, woods, valleys, heathland, commons, and London Green Belt countryside. Sights: [Down House](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/server.php?show=nav.14922), Keston Ponds, Caesar's Well, Wilberforce Oak, Holwood House, Downe Bank Nature Reserve, St Peter and St Paul Church, Hayes Common. Can be waterlogged. Lunch at the [Blacksmith's Arms](http://www.theblacksmithsarms.co.uk/) in Cudham, the Queens Head in Downe, or the George & Dragon in Downe. 16 km.

**[Knockholt Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-circular/)**: Rural fields with wildflowers, bluebell woods, and several village churches. Sights: [Down House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/home-of-charles-darwin-down-house/). Numerous stiles throughout. Best in late April/May. Lunch at the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheaddowne.com/) in Downe, the [George & Dragon](https://www.georgeanddragondowne.com/) in Downe, or the [Blacksmiths Arms](https://www.theblacksmithsarmscudham.com/) in Cudham. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Knockholt to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-to-otford/)**: Wooded hills, steep ridges, hidden downland valley, bluebell woods, beech and sweet chestnut woodland, fields. Sights: Magpie Bottom, Shoreham Aircraft Museum, Mount Vineyard. Descents can be slippery. Best in spring or autumn. Lunch at the Kings Arms in Shoreham, the Samuel Palmer in Shoreham, or the Crown in Shoreham. 12 km.

**[Caterham to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/caterham-to-knockholt/)**: North Downs escarpment, woods carpeted with wild garlic and bluebells, open pasture, arable fields, fine southward views, some M25 road noise early on. Sights: [Titsey Place](http://www.titsey.org/), [North Downs Way](http://www.nationaltrail.co.uk/northdowns/), [Botley Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Botley_Hill), Oxted Downs, Chevening House, Titsey Brewing Taproom. Can be slippery. Lunch at the [Godstone Vineyards](http://www.godstonevineyards.uk/) in Caterham area, the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk/) in Botley Hill, or the [Titsey Brewing Taproom](https://titseybrewingco.com/taproom/) in Titsey. 26 km.

0.1313783,50.9901183.name: Buxted
0.1313783,50.9901183.ramblerNote:
**Buxted to Ashurst**: an alternative variant of the [Ashurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-circular/) walk. River valleys, woodland, elevated Ashdown Forest heathland, Scots pine clumps, Wealden farmland, and villages. Sights: [Ashdown Forest](https://ashdownforest.org/walking), Gills Lap Clump, King's Standing Clump, Heffalump Trap, Pooh Sticks Bridge, [Pooh Corner](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk), [Piglet's Tearoom](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk/tearoom), St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, Linkhorns Farmhouse, Holy Trinity Church, St Mary the Virgin. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Crow & Gate Inn](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thecrowandgatecrowborough) in Poundgate. 24 km. 6 hours.

**[Buxted Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxted-circular/)**: Gentle mix of fields and woods, Weald countryside, views of the South Downs ridge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Crow and Gate in Poundgate. 16 km.

**[Uckfield to Buxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-buxted/)**: Undulating Low Weald countryside, Uck Valley, golf course, stream valleys, old deer park, parkland, and bluebell woods. Sights: East Sussex National Golf Course, High Cross Estate, Tickerage Mill, Buxted Park, Buxted House. Can be muddy. Best in bluebell season. Lunch at the [Blackboys Inn](http://www.theblackboys.co.uk/) in Blackboys. 21 km. 5 hours.

**[Cambridge via Grantchester Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cambridge-via-grantchester-circular/)**: Flat city streets, college courts, riverside meadow paths, surfaced cycle paths, and quiet village lanes. Sights: King's College Chapel, King's College, Clare College, Trinity College, Trinity Hall, St John's College, Gonville and Caius College, Sidney Sussex College, Pembroke College, Corpus Christi College, Peterhouse, Wren Library, Clare Bridge, Gate of Honour, St Bene't's Church, Holy Trinity Church, Great St Mary's Church, Church of St Edward, Little St Mary's Church, Church of St Andrew and St Mary, Old Vicarage, Rupert Brooke Museum, Orchard Tea Rooms, Cambridge University Botanic Gardens, Byron's Pool, Eagle pub, Old Cavendish Lab. 15 km. 5 hours.

Sights: Church of St Mary the Virgin, All Saints Church Little Shelford, Church of St Edmunds Hauxton, All Saints Church Haslingfield, Travelling Telescope, Parish Church of St Andrew and St Mary Grantchester, Orchard Tea Rooms, Old Vicarage Grantchester, Rupert Brooke statue, Queens College, King's College, Clare College, Wren Library, Clare Bridge, Trinity Hall, Gate of Honour, Gonville and Caius College, Trinity College, St John's College, Sidney Sussex College, Great St Mary's Church, Church of St Edward, Saint Bene't's Church, Eagle pub, Corpus Christi College, Old Cavendish Lab, Pembroke College, Peterhouse, Little St Mary's Church, Fitzwilliam Museum, Cambridge University Botanic Gardens. 0.1526024,51.1284231.name: Ashurst
0.1526024,51.1284231.ramblerNote:
**[Ashurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-circular/)**: Low hills, Medway valley, ancient woodland, Wealden farmland, Five Hundred Acre Wood, wildflower meadows, and quaint villages. Sights: [St Michael and All Angels](https://www.withyhamchurch.org/history), [Pooh Corner](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk), Pooh Sticks Bridge, Five Hundred Acre Wood, [Piglet's Tearoom](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk/tearoom), Old Buckhurst, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, Linkhorns Farmhouse. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk) in Withyham. 20 km. 5 hours.

**Ashurst to East Grinstead**: a longer variant of the [Ashurst to Forest Row and East Grinstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-forest-row-and-east-grinstead/) walk. Low hills, Wealden farmland, wooded bridleways, open Ashdown Forest heathland, golf course, disused railway trackbed (Forest Way), and historic town streets. Sights: [Ashdown Forest](https://ashdownforest.org/walking), Ashdown Forest Centre, [Forest Way](https://www.eastsussex.gov.uk/leisureandtourism/countryside/walks/forestway), [East Grinstead](http://www.westsussex.info/east-grinstead.shtml), Newbridge Mill, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, Pooh Corner, Gallipot Inn, Brambletye House, Sackville College, St Swithun, Monument to Sir Archibald McIndoe, Freshfield Hall, Brambletye Hotel. Can be muddy. Best in Oct/Nov. Lunch at the [Gallipot Inn](https://www.the-gallipot-inn.co.uk) in Gallipot Street, the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com) in Hartfield, or the [Hatch Inn](https://www.hatchinn.co.uk) in Colemans Hatch. 22 km. 5.5 hours.

**Ashurst to Eridge**: a shorter variant of the [Eridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-circular/) walk. High Weald valleys, bluebell woods, Burrswood estate, meadows, heathland, and nature reserves; omits Harrison's Rocks loop. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), Burrswood, St John the Evangelist, Groombridge station. Can be muddy. Best in spring. Lunch at the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrowngroombridge.com) in Groombridge, or the Junction Inn in Groombridge. 12 km.

**[Ashurst to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/)**: Low hills, steep-sided valleys, field edges, country lanes, wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, heath, and meadows. Sights: [Speldhurst](https://www.speldhurst.org/history), [St Mary the Virgin](https://speldhurstchurch.org/about/building/history), [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, Ashurst Place, Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [George & Dragon](https://ganddspeldhurst.com) in Speldhurst. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Hever to Ashurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-ashurst/)**: Low hills, wooded valleys, farm fields, bluebell woods, manor house grounds, golf course, orchards, and remote High Weald countryside. Sights: Crippenden Manor, Leighton Manor, Waystrode Manor, Sussex Border Path, Sussex House Farm, Heathersome's Wood, Bolebroke Castle, [Orchard Tea Room](https://www.perryhillorchards.co.uk/orchard-tea-room), St Mary Magdalene, [Cowden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cowden). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaincowden.co.uk) in Cowden. 16 km. 4 hours.

Sights: [Henry Moore Foundation](https://henry-moore.org/studios-and-gardens/). Cattle in pastures. Avoid end Oct–Easter. 0.1657244,50.8404862.ramblerNote:
**[Berwick to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-eastbourne/)**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, reservoir, chalk downland, ridge grassland, chalk figure, smugglers villages, seaside resort. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Arlington Reservoir, Filching Manor Motor Museum. Lunch at the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/) in Wilmington, or the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/) in Jevington. 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/)**: South Downs chalk hills, open downland, river valley, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and picturesque village. Sights: Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, High and Over Hill, Berwick church, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Long Man Inn in Wilmington, the George Inn in Alfriston, the Cuckmere Inn in Exceat, the Trawlers in Seaford, the Plough Inn in Seaford, or the Steamworks in Seaford. 20 km.

**[Berwick via Upper Dicker Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-via-upper-dicker-circular/)**: Gently undulating farmland, open fields, bluebell woods, reservoir shorepath, and quiet country lanes with fine downs and weald views. Sights: [Michelham Priory](https://sussexpast.co.uk/attraction/michelham-priory/), Abbot's Wood, Arlington Reservoir, Long Man of Wilmington, Holy Trinity Church. Can be slippery. Lunch at the [Old Oak Inn](https://www.oldoakinnarlington.co.uk/) in Arlington, the [Blue Door Cafe](https://bluedoorcafe.co.uk/) in Michelham Priory, or the [Plough](https://www.plough-upperdicker.co.uk/) in Upper Dicker. 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**Bishopstone to Berwick**: a shorter variant of the [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/) walk. Downland climbs, South Downs Way, descent to Berwick via Vanguard Way. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow, St Michael and All Angels church. Can be overgrown. Lunch at the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/) in Berwick. 13 km.

0.1731809,51.1972733.ramblerNote:
**Hever to Penshurst**: an alternative variant of the [Hever to Leigh](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-leigh/) walk. Fields, woods, and country lanes across to Penshurst Station. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), Chiddingstone Castle, St Mary's Church. Lunch at the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/) in Chiddingstone.

**Hever to Penshurst**: a shorter variant of the [Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/) walk. Farmland, river valley, parkland, and historic village. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, Withers. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst. 12 km. 3 hours.

**[Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/)**: Rambler favourite! Parkland, river valley, gentle farmland slopes, vineyard, woods, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), St John the Baptist, Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 13 km. 3 hours.

**[Penshurst Circular via Bough Beech](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/penshurst-circular/)**: Low-lying farmland, woodland, reservoir causeway, field edges, river meadows, and attractive Kent villages. Sights: [Bore Place](https://www.boreplace.org/visit/walks-at-bore-place), Bough Beech Reservoir, [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Chiding Stone, St Luke, Chiddingstone Causeway, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Larkin's Brewery, Penshurst Platform Gallery, Whistler's Farmhouse, Penshurst Airfield. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Wheatsheaf](https://www.wheatsheafboughbeech.co.uk) in Bough Beech, or the [Castle Inn](https://thecastleinnchiddingstone.co.uk) in Chiddingstone. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/)**: Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, orchards, vineyards, farmland, and picturesque villages. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Harden Vineyard, Larkin's Brewery, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 18 km. 4 hours.

0.1819438,51.2764117.name: Sevenoaks
0.1819438,51.2764117.publicNote: **[Sevenoaks to Borough Green, via Ightham Mote](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902569123)**: Nonstop beautiful landscape with [Ightham Mote](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/ightham-mote) — a small but charming 14th Century National Trust manor — in the middle, where you can have lunch and easily fit in a tour of the house. [Knole House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/knole) at the Sevenoaks end of the walk is well worth a visit but requires an entire day in itself. Adapted from The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East, 3rd edition.

0.1819438,51.2764117.ramblerNote:
**[Yalding to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-sevenoaks/)**: Rambler favourite! Undulating Kent farmland, orchards, bluebell woods, greensand escarpment, and deer park. Sights: Ightham Mote, Knole House, One Tree Hill. Waymarking can disappear. Best in late Apr/May. Lunch at the Swan in West Peckham, the Kentish Rifleman in Dunks Green, or the Chaser Inn in Shipbourne. 21 km.

**Edenbridge to Sevenoaks**: an alternative variant of the [Hurst Green to Chiddingstone Causeway](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-chiddingstone-causeway/) walk. Open fields, NE ascent to Toy's Hill, easterly woodland walking after Ide Hill. Sights: Toy's Hill, Ide Hill. Lunch at the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnidehill.co.uk/) in Ide Hill. 17 km.

**[Leigh to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-to-sevenoaks/)**: Rolling Kentish farmland, fields, oasthouses, bluebell woods, steep greensand hillside, deer park. Sights: St Mary's Church, Hall Place, Knole Park, Knole House, Brewhouse Tearoom. Can be muddy. Lunch at the White Rock in Underriver. 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Sevenoaks Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sevenoaks-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! Deer park, fields, woods, coppiced woodland, escarpment ridge, bluebell and wild garlic woods, Weald views. Sights: Knole House, Knole Park, Ightham Mote, One Tree Hill. Some mud inevitable. Best in late Apr/May. Lunch at the Brewhouse Cafe in Knole House, the Bucks Head in Godden Green, or the Ightham Mote restaurant in Ightham Mote. 16 km.

**[Hurst Green to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-sevenoaks/)**: Greensand ridge, chart hills, undulating woodland, commons, deer park, historic estates, suburban old town. Sights: Chartwell, Toy's Hill, Ide Hill, Knole Park, Knole Palace. Steep climbs. Lunch at the NT restaurant at Chartwell in Chartwell, or the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxhoundstoyshill.co.uk/) in Toys Hill. 18 km.

0.1887766,51.3321241.ramblerNote:
**Eynsford to Shoreham**: a shorter variant of the [Eynsford Circular via Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-otford/) walk. Rambler favourite! Secluded valleys, chalk downland ridges, woodland, chalk grassland nature reserves, and riverside village paths. Sights: [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bull](https://thebull-otford.co.uk) in Otford, or the [Olive Tree](https://hospicesofhope.co.uk/our-shops/#our-bistros) in Otford. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Farningham Road to Shoreham**: a shorter variant of the [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/) walk. Ancient woodland, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, quaint village of Shoreham. Sights: [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), Lullingstone Castle, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, The Mount Vineyard. Motocross path closures possible. Lunch at the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham) in Farningham, or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk) in Farningham. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Knockholt to Shoreham**: a shorter variant of the [Knockholt to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-to-otford/) walk. Wooded hills, steep ridge climb, descent into village. Sights: Shoreham Aircraft Museum. Descent can be slippery. Lunch at the Kings Arms in Shoreham, the Samuel Palmer in Shoreham, or the Crown in Shoreham. 5 km.

**Otford to Shoreham**: a shorter variant of the [Otford Circular via Romney Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-circular/) walk. Rambler favourite! North Downs hills, chalk woodland, bluebell woods, open commons, grassy fields, and Shoreham village. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul, Shoreham Memorial Cross. Can be muddy. Lunch at the King's Arms in Shoreham, the Crown in Shoreham, or the Samuel Palmer in Shoreham. 8 km.

0.1932286,52.0599283.ramblerNote: **Great Chesterford to Audley End**: a longer variant of the [Great Chesterford to Newport](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-chesterford-to-newport/) walk. Rolling Essex farmland, grassy field edges, small woods, river valleys, historic market town, parkland, village with thatched cottages. Sights: All Saints Church, Great Chesterford, St Mary the Virgin, Little Chesterford, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, Monk's Barn, St Mary the Virgin, Wendens Ambo. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Eight Bells](https://www.theeightbellssaffronwalden.com) in Saffron Walden, the Kings Arms in Saffron Walden, the [Cross Keys Hotel](https://www.theoldcrosskeys.co.uk) in Saffron Walden, or the [Temeraire](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-temeraire-saffron-walden) in Saffron Walden. 20 km. 5 hours.

0.1971759,51.3129318.name: Otford
0.1971759,51.3129318.ramblerNote:
**[Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/)**: Ancient woodland, field edges, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, chalk downland, quaint villages, golf course, nature reserves. Sights: [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), South Darenth Viaduct, Franks Hall, Farningham Cattle Screen, Farningham Mill, Ss Peter and Paul, Farningham, St Martin of Tours, Lullingstone Castle, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, The Mount Vineyard, White Hill Nature Reserve, Fackenden Down, Otford Solar System, Otford Pond, St Bartholomew, Otford, Otford Palace, Kings Arms, Water House. Motocross path closures possible. Lunch at the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham) in Farningham, the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk) in Farningham, the [Castle Hotel](https://www.castlehotelkent.com) in Eynsford, the [Malt Shovel](https://themaltshoveleynsford.co.uk) in Eynsford, or the [Plough Inn](https://www.theploughinneynsford.co.uk) in Eynsford. 20 km. 5 hours.

**[Knockholt to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-to-otford/)**: Wooded hills, steep ridges, hidden downland valley, bluebell woods, beech and sweet chestnut woodland, fields. Sights: Magpie Bottom, Shoreham Aircraft Museum, Mount Vineyard. Descents can be slippery. Best in spring or autumn. Lunch at the Kings Arms in Shoreham, the Samuel Palmer in Shoreham, or the Crown in Shoreham. 12 km.

**[Otford to Snodland](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-snodland/)**: North Downs ridge, southern escarpment, fields, woods, open views south, forest, country park, open countryside, urban streets. Sights: Trosley Country Park. Lunch at the [Rose and Crown](https://www.roseandcrownwrotham.co.uk/) in Wrotham, or the Village in Vigo Village. 21 km.

**[Otford Circular via Romney Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! North Downs hills, chalk woodland, bluebell woods, open commons, grassy fields, disused golf course, Darent Valley riverbank, and quaint village. Sights: [The Ancients](http://www.tate.org.uk/collections/glossary/definition.jsp?entryId=332), [Pre-Raphaelite](http://www.tate.org.uk/collections/glossary/definition.jsp?entryId=231), Church of St Peter and St Paul, Otford Palace, Otford Solar System. Can be muddy. Best in bluebell season. Lunch at the Rising Sun in Cotman's Ash, the King's Arms in Shoreham, the Crown in Shoreham, the Samuel Palmer in Shoreham, the Shoreham Aircraft Museum Tea Room in Shoreham, the Honey Pot in Shoreham, or the Mount Vineyard in Shoreham. 12 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Otford Circular via Shoreham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-circular-via-shoreham/)**: Steep-sided hills, secluded chalk valleys, mixed woodland, open countryside, chalk grassland nature reserves, and attractive villages. Sights: Otford Chalk Pit, North Downs Way, Magpie Bottom, Ss Peter and Paul, [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), War Memorial, Water House, [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk), Shoreham Aircraft Museum, Polhill Bank, Shoreham Cross, [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), Broughton Manor, Pickmoss, Otford Pond, St Bartholomew, Otford Palace. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com) in Shoreham, the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk) in Shoreham, the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk) in Shoreham, or the Crown in Shoreham. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Otford to Eynsford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-eynsford/)**: River valley, open fields, orchards, beech woods, golf course, ridge path, historic villages. Sights: St Bartholomew's Church, Otford Palace, Otford Solar System, Church of St Peter and St Paul, Shoreham Memorial Cross, Lullingstone Park Visitor Centre, [Lullingstone Castle](http://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk/), [Lullingstone Roman Villa](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/lullingstone-roman-villa/), Eagle Heights Wildlife Foundation, [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle/), St Martin's Church, Eynsford Viaduct. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [King's Arms](https://www.facebook.com/thekingsarmsshoreham/) in Shoreham, the [Crown](https://www.thecrownshoreham.co.uk/) in Shoreham, or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com/) in Shoreham. 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Eridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-circular/)**: Undulating High Weald, streams, meadows, sandstone outcrops, heathland, conifer-to-heathland nature reserve, and woods. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), St Thomas the Apostle, St John the Evangelist, Groombridge station, Birchden Forge, Wildwood Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrowngroombridge.com) in Groombridge, or the Junction Inn in Groombridge. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Eridge to East Grinstead**: a longer variant of the [Eridge to Forest Row and East Grinstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-to-forest-row-and-east-grinstead/) walk. Undulating High Weald countryside, clay paths, woods, fields, estate grounds, river valley, disused railway cycleway, hilltop town streets. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [High Weald Landscape Trail](https://highweald.org/things-to-do/high-weald-landscape-trail), Buckhurst Park, Penns in the Rocks, [St Michael and All Angels](https://www.withyhamchurch.org/history), Old Buckhurst, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, [Pooh Corner](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk), [Tablehurst Farm](https://www.tablehurstfarm.org.uk), [Forest Way](https://www.eastsussex.gov.uk/leisureandtourism/countryside/walks/forestway), Brambletye House, Sackville College, St Swithun's Church, [East Grinstead](http://www.westsussex.info/east-grinstead.shtml). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk) in Withyham, or the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com) in Hartfield. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Ashurst to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/)**: Low hills, steep-sided valleys, field edges, country lanes, wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, heath, and meadows. Sights: [Speldhurst](https://www.speldhurst.org/history), [St Mary the Virgin](https://speldhurstchurch.org/about/building/history), [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, Ashurst Place, Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [George & Dragon](https://ganddspeldhurst.com) in Speldhurst. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Cowden to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-eridge/)**: Weald pasture, woods, hills, undulating fields, and valley paths. Sights: Pooh Corner, Old Buckhurst, Church of St Michael and All Angels. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com/) in Hartfield, or the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk/) in Withyham. 21 km. 6 hours.

**[Eynsford Circular via Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-otford/)**: Rambler favourite! **[Otford to Eynsford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-eynsford/)**: River valley, open fields, orchards, beech woods, golf course, ridge path, historic villages. Sights: St Bartholomew's Church, Otford Palace, Otford Solar System, Church of St Peter and St Paul, Shoreham Memorial Cross, Lullingstone Park Visitor Centre, [Lullingstone Castle](http://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk/), [Lullingstone Roman Villa](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/lullingstone-roman-villa/), Eagle Heights Wildlife Foundation, [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle/), St Martin's Church, Eynsford Viaduct. **Great Chesterford to Audley End**: a longer variant of the [Great Chesterford to Newport](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-chesterford-to-newport/) walk. Sights: All Saints Church, Great Chesterford, St Mary the Virgin, Little Chesterford, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, Monk's Barn, St Mary the Virgin, Wendens Ambo. **[Wendens Ambo Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wendens-ambo-circular/)**: Gently rolling chalky uplands, farmland, woods and copses, green lanes, field boundaries, pretty villages with thatched cottages and pargeted houses. Sights: St. Mary the Virgin, Arkesden, The Cottage (pargeted house, Wendens Ambo). Lunch at the [Axe & Compasses](http://www.axeandcompasses.co.uk/) in Arkesden, or the [Red Cow](http://www.theredcow.com/) in Chrishall. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Hever to Leigh](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-leigh/)**: Undulating hills, open fields, beech woods, rivers, ponds, quaint villages, and historic estates. **Leigh to Tonbridge**: a shorter variant of the [Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/) walk. Parkland estate, river valley, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Straight Mile, Powder Mills. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst. 12 km. 3 hours.

**[Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/)**: Rambler favourite! Parkland, river valley, gentle farmland slopes, vineyard, woods, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), St John the Baptist, Harden Vineyard. **[Leigh to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-to-sevenoaks/)**: Rolling Kentish farmland, fields, oasthouses, bluebell woods, steep greensand hillside, deer park. Sights: St Mary's Church, Hall Place, Knole Park, Knole House, Brewhouse Tearoom. Can be muddy. Lunch at the White Rock in Underriver. 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**Tunbridge Wells to Leigh**: an alternative variant of the [Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/) walk. Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river and lake paths, quiet roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/) in Bidborough. 14 km.

**Hildenborough to Tonbridge**: a longer variant of the [Hildenborough to Hadlow or Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hildenborough-to-hadlow-or-tonbridge/) walk. Wealden countryside, meadows, farm fields, woods, long riverside path along the River Medway into Tonbridge. Sights: [Hadlow Tower](http://www.thehadlowtower.co.uk/), [Broadview Gardens](https://www.hadlow.ac.uk/about-us/broadview-gardens-and-garden-centre), St Giles, Shipbourne, Hadlow College, St Mary, Hadlow, Hadlow Castle, Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Sights: Hall Place, St Mary, Leigh, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Harden Vineyard, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, Penshurst Platform Gallery. **[Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/)**: Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river paths, and quiet roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/) in Bidborough, or the [Plough at Leigh](http://www.theploughatleigh.com/) in Hildenborough. 17 km.

Lunch at the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/) in Bidborough, or the [Plough at Leigh](http://www.theploughatleigh.com/) in Hildenborough. 17 km.

0.2664989,52.3906885.name: Ely
0.2664989,52.3906885.ramblerNote: **[Ely Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ely-circular/)**: Open fenland, flat drained farmland, long straight tree-lined farm tracks, hedge-lined meadows, river banks, dykes and catchwater drains, quayside, historic city. Sights: [Ely Cathedral](http://www.elycathedral.org/), [Cromwell's House](http://www.olivercromwellshouse.co.uk/). Exposed river banks. Lunch at the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionstretham.co.uk/) in Stretham. 18 km. 4 hours.

0.2699969,51.1915091.name: Tonbridge
0.2699969,51.1915091.ramblerNote:
**Hever to Tonbridge**: a longer variant of the [Hever to Leigh](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-leigh/) walk. Undulating hills, open fields, rivers, Haysden Country Park, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), Chiddingstone Castle, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), Haysden Country Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/) in Chiddingstone, or the [Leicester Arms Hotel](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/) in Penshurst.

**[Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/)**: Gentle farmland, orchards, river valleys, parkland, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, Withers, Straight Mile, Powder Mills. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst. 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Hildenborough to Tonbridge**: a longer variant of the [Hildenborough to Hadlow or Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hildenborough-to-hadlow-or-tonbridge/) walk. Wealden countryside, meadows, farm fields, woods, long riverside path along the River Medway into Tonbridge. Sights: [Hadlow Tower](http://www.thehadlowtower.co.uk/), [Broadview Gardens](https://www.hadlow.ac.uk/about-us/broadview-gardens-and-garden-centre), St Giles, Shipbourne, Hadlow College, St Mary, Hadlow, Hadlow Castle, Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Chaser Inn](https://www.thechaser.co.uk/) in Shipbourne, or the [Kentish Rifleman](https://www.thekentishrifleman.co.uk/) in Dunk's Green. 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-circular-via-tudeley/)**: Parkland, farm fields, paddocks, orchards, country park, riverside paths, residential streets, and fishing and sailing lakes. Sights: [All Saints Church, Tudeley](https://www.tudeley.org/Groups/363846/Chagall_Windows.aspx), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), Tonbridge Castle, Somerhill House, Hadlow Tower, Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Stone Lock, Heusenstamm Wood, Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Lunch at the [Poacher & Partridge](https://elitepubs.com/venue/the-poacher-and-partridge) in Tudeley Hale. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/)**: Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, orchards, vineyards, farmland, and picturesque villages. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Harden Vineyard, Larkin's Brewery, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 18 km. 4 hours.

**Tunbridge Wells to Tonbridge**: an alternative variant of the [Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/) walk. Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river and lake paths, urban roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/) in Bidborough. 14 km.

0.2796796,50.7959254.name: Hampden Park
0.2796796,50.7959254.ramblerNote: **[Hampden Park to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampden-park-to-eastbourne/)**: Parks, woods, chalk downland ridges, open grassland, chalk heath, dense beech forest, coastal farmland, golf course, and Victorian seaside town. Sights: Hampden Park, Lullington Heath National Nature Reserve, Friston Forest, St Andrew's Church, Jevington, Friston Place, Belle Tout Lighthouse, Birling Gap, Eastbourne cricket ground, Eastbourne Town Hall, Gildredge Park, Little Chelsea. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/) in Jevington, the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/) in East Dean, the Hikers' Rest Coffee Shop in East Dean, the Thai Terre in East Dean, or the Beehive on the Green in East Dean. 26 km.

0.2798724,51.4504764.name: Greenhithe
0.2798724,51.4504764.ramblerNote: **[Dartford to Greenhithe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dartford-to-greenhithe/)**: Flat riverside paths, flood defences, marshland, industrial edge-land, and wide Thames estuary views. Sights: Queen Elizabeth Bridge, Dartford Marshes, Littlebrook Power Station, [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk — check warnings. Lunch at the Asda café in Greenhithe. 11 km.

0.2815355,50.7696435.name: Eastbourne
0.2815355,50.7696435.publicNote: For strong walkers I highly recommend walking all the way from Eastbourne to Seaford Station. This starts out following the charming Eastbourne seafront and then into the absolutely spectacular Seven Sisters cliff walk. You can have lunch at the National Trust village of Birling Gap, before completing the trek through the marshes of Cuckmere Haven. It's long but impossible to mess up since you're just following the sea, and everything is signposted. The cliffs are best appreciated when sparkling on a bright summer's day. Alas, everyone else knows this so the Seven Sisters part is extremely busy on Summer weekends — although it does create a jolly festival atmosphere if you can tolerate that. To me this is the greatest walk in England.
0.2815355,50.7696435.privateNote:
-- Rambler-walk issues (auto-generated) --
[berwick-to-birling-gap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-birling-gap/): All walk variants end at non-station points (Birling Gap, Friston, East Dean, Exceat) requiring bus to Eastbourne or Seaford — none are station-to-station walks. The page does not give pure walking hours for any variant.
[eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill/): The page mentions 'Pevensey' as a walk start, but there are two Pevensey stations: Pevensey & Westham and Pevensey Bay — the page refers to the inland one (Pevensey & Westham) for the shorter variant starting at Pevensey, but does not make this entirely explicit. | Start "Norman's Bay" not found in stations | Start "Norman's Bay" not found in stations | Start "Pevensey" is ambiguous (candidates: Pevensey Bay, Pevensey and Westham)
-- end Rambler-walk issues --
0.2815355,50.7696435.ramblerNote:
**[Berwick to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-eastbourne/)**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, reservoir, chalk downland, ridge grassland, chalk figure, smugglers villages, seaside resort. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Arlington Reservoir, Filching Manor Motor Museum. Lunch at the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/) in Wilmington, or the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/) in Jevington. 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**Eastbourne circular**: an alternative variant of the [Eastbourne to East Dean](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-east-dean/) walk. Seafront promenade, chalk cliffs, open downs, quiet downland valley, ridge descent, town streets. Sights: Beachy Head, Belle Tout, Birling Gap, Seven Sisters. Mile on Eastbourne streets. Lunch at the Beachy Head pub in Beachy Head, the Birling Gap National Trust Tea Room in Birling Gap, the Tiger Inn in East Dean, or the Hiker's Rest in East Dean. 17 km.

**[Eastbourne to Hastings via Bexhill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill/)**: Flat coastal seafront promenades, shingle beach, sandy beach at low tide, sea wall paths, quiet coast roads, low cliffs. Sights: [Eastbourne Pier](http://www.eastbournepier.com/), [Eastbourne Harbour](http://www.eastbourneharbour.com/walks.php), [Martello Towers](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martello_tower), [Pevensey Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/daysout/properties/pevensey-castle/), [De La Warr Pavilion](http://www.dlwp.com/), Hastings Pier. Shingle heavy going. Lunch at the Aqua Bar in Pevensey Bay, the Moorings in Pevensey Bay, the [Castle Inn](http://castleinnpevenseybay.co.uk/) in Pevensey Bay, the [Star Inn](https://www.thestarinnnormansbay.co.uk/) in Norman's Bay, the [Relais Cooden Beach](https://www.therelaisretreats.com/coodenbeach/) in Cooden Beach, the [De La Warr Pavilion Cafe, Bar & Kitchen](http://www.dlwp.com/) in Bexhill, the [Farmyard](https://farmyardwine.com/) in St Leonard's, or the [Royal](http://theroyalstleonards.co.uk/) in St Leonard's. 26 km.

**[Hampden Park to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampden-park-to-eastbourne/)**: Parks, woods, chalk downland ridges, open grassland, chalk heath, dense beech forest, coastal farmland, golf course, and Victorian seaside town. Sights: Hampden Park, Lullington Heath National Nature Reserve, Friston Forest, St Andrew's Church, Jevington, Friston Place, Belle Tout Lighthouse, Birling Gap, Eastbourne cricket ground, Eastbourne Town Hall, Gildredge Park, Little Chelsea. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/) in Jevington, the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/) in East Dean, the Hikers' Rest Coffee Shop in East Dean, the Thai Terre in East Dean, or the Beehive on the Green in East Dean. 26 km.

**Polegate to Eastbourne**: a longer variant of the [Polegate to Birling Gap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/polegate-to-birling-gap/) walk. South Downs downland, coastal clifftop path from Birling Gap eastward to Eastbourne. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/) in East Dean, or the [Deli on the Green](https://www.deli-onthegreen.com/) in East Dean.

**[Seaford to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-to-eastbourne/)**: Rambler favourite! Dramatic clifftop chalk downs, undulating Seven Sisters cliffs, shingle beaches, river haven, elegant seafront promenade. Sights: [Birling Gap](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/birling-gap-and-the-seven-sisters), Seven Sisters, Belle Tout, Martello Tower. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the Cuckmere Inn in Exceat, the Saltmarsh Kitchen in Exceat, or the Grab and Go in Exceat. 21 km.

**[Southease to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/)**: Open treeless downland ridges, steep ascents, scenic Cuckmere Valley, coastal promenade, beach, and pier. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, Beachy Head, Wish Tower, Eastbourne Pier, Italian Gardens. Steep ascents. Lunch at the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop in Alfriston. 27 km.

0.2943374,51.1040897.name: Frant
0.2943374,51.1040897.ramblerNote:
**Wadhurst to Frant**: a shorter variant of the [Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/) walk. High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, descent through woodland, brief roadside stretch. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, [Brecknock Arms](https://www.thebrecknockarmspub.com). No pavement on final road. Lunch at the George Inn in Frant, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk) in Frant. 11 km. 3 hours.

**Frant to Tunbridge Wells**: a shorter variant of the [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/) walk. High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), The Pantiles, High Rocks, Eridge Old Park. Busy road at start. Best in spring. Lunch at the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun) in Eridge Green. 17 km.

0.3130406,51.0733883.name: Wadhurst
0.3130406,51.0733883.ramblerNote:
**[Etchingham to Wadhurst via Ticehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/etchingham-to-wadhurst-via-ticehurst/)**: Hilly Wealden pasture and woodland, panoramic valley views, converted oast houses, cobnut orchard, back lanes with wildflower verges, reservoir shoreline. Sights: Bewl Water, Ticehurst village, Wadhurst village. Can be muddy. Best in spring. Lunch at the Bell in Ticehurst, or the Bull Inn in Three Leg Cross. 17 km.

**[Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/)**: High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, landscaped parkland, back streets and alleyways of a spa town. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), [Tunbridge Wells](https://www.visittunbridgewells.com/explore), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, Nevill Crest, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, [Juliets](https://www.julietsandmore.com), [Opera House](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/opera-house-royal-tunbridge-wells). Can be boggy. Best in late autumn. Lunch at the George Inn in Frant, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk) in Frant. 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Wadhurst via Bewl Water Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-via-bewl-water-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! Hidden valleys, ancient woods, quaint old farms, reservoir shoreline, hilltop village, gentle but rolling Wealden landscape. Sights: Bewl Water Reservoir. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Old Vine in Cousley Wood. 17 km.

**Stonegate to Wadhurst**: an alternative variant of the [Stonegate Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-circular/) walk. Rolling hills, tranquil valleys, woodland, pastureland, remote wilderness sections. Sights: [Bateman's](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk), Church of St. Bartholomew. Nettles and brambles. Lunch at the [Wheel Inn](http://www.thewheelburwashweald.co.uk/) in Burwash Weald. 32 km.

**[Wadhurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-circular/)**: High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, hedgerows, quiet farmland, historic villages. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul, [Bewl Water](http://www.bewl.co.uk), Church of Saint Dunstans. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rose & Crown Inn](http://www.roseandcrownmayfield.co.uk/) in Mayfield, the [Middle House](http://www.themiddlehousemayfield.co.uk/) in Mayfield, or the [Greyhound](http://www.thegreyhoundwadhurst.co.uk/) in Wadhurst. 19 km. 6 hours.

0.3538289,51.4624598.name: Tilbury Town
0.3538289,51.4624598.ramblerNote: **[Tilbury to East Tilbury via the 2 Forts Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tilbury-to-east-tilbury-via-2-forts-way/)**: Flat Thames Estuary coast path, riverside sea wall, wild flower meadows, salt marsh views, former landfill landscape, brief inland section. Sights: [Tilbury Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tilbury_Fort), [Coalhouse Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coalhouse_Fort), [Thurrock Thameside Nature Park](https://www.essexwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/thurrock-thameside), St Catherine's Church, East Tilbury Radar Tower. Check tide timings. Lunch at the World's End in Tilbury Fort, or the [Ship](https://theshipeasttilburyvillage.co.uk) in Coalhouse Fort. 13 km.

0.3566218,51.3864534.name: Meopham
0.3566218,51.3864534.ramblerNote: **[Meopham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/meopham-to-rochester/)**: Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, community woodland, landscaped parkland, wildflower meadows, farm fields, country park, and busy road stretches. Sights: [Jeskyns Community Woodland](https://www.forestryengland.uk/jeskyns), [Ashenbank Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/ashenbank-wood), [Cobham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cobham,_Kent), [Cobham Park (Cobham Wood and Mausoleum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/cobham-wood-and-mausoleum), Darnley Mausoleum, [Ranscombe Farm Nature Reserve](https://www.plantlife.org.uk/our-work/ranscombe-farm-nature-reserve), [Camer Park](https://explorekent.org/activities/camer-park), St Mary Magdalene Church, New College of Cobham, Nurstead Court, Bronze Age Barrow, Cobham Hall, Luddesdown Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leather Bottle](https://www.theleatherbottle.pub/index) in Cobham, the Darnley Arms in Cobham, or the [Ship Inn](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/kent/ship-inn) in Cobham. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

0.363387,51.0201404.name: Stonegate
0.363387,51.0201404.ramblerNote:
**Robertsbridge to Stonegate**: an alternative variant of the [Robertsbridge Circular via Brightling](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/robertsbridge-circular-via-brightling/) walk. East Sussex countryside, rolling hills, fields, and woodland via Burwash to Stonegate. Lunch at the Wheel Inn in Burwash. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Stonegate to Robertsbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-to-robertsbridge/)**: Hilly High Weald, valleys, ridgetop village, hornbeam woods, country estate, panoramic ridge views. Sights: Batemans. Can be muddy. Best in April or Oct/Nov. Lunch at the Rose & Crown in Burwash, the Bear Inn in Burwash, the Blacksmiths Tea and Coffee Shop in Burwash, the Ostrich in Robertsbridge, or the George Inn in Robertsbridge. 14 km.

**[Stonegate Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-circular/)**: Rolling hills, tranquil valleys, classic English woodland, pastureland, unspoiled village. Sights: [Bateman's](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk), Church of St. Bartholomew. Nettles and brambles. Lunch at the Rose and Crown in Burwash, the [Bear Inn](http://www.bear-inn-hotel-burwash.co.uk/) in Burwash, or the [Wheel Inn](http://www.thewheelburwashweald.co.uk/) in Burwash Weald. 15 km. 5 hours.

**Wadhurst to Stonegate**: an alternative variant of the [Wadhurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-circular/) walk. High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, quiet farmland, historic villages. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul, Church of Saint Dunstans. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rose & Crown Inn](http://www.roseandcrownmayfield.co.uk/) in Mayfield. 16 km.

0.3668306,51.4413627.name: Gravesend
0.3668306,51.4413627.ramblerNote: **[Higham to Gravesend](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/higham-to-gravesend/)**: Flat estuary landscape, gravel tracks, grassy paths, marshes, open water, mud flats, RSPB reserve, industrial heritage, big skies. Sights: [Cliffe Pools Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/c/cliffepools/about.aspx), [St Helen's Church Cliffe](http://www.cliffehistory.co.uk/martin.html), Cliffe Fort, St Marys Church Lower Higham, Brennan Torpedo Tracks. Can be muddy. Best in autumn or spring. 18 km.

0.3781687,51.383247.name: Sole Street
0.3781687,51.383247.ramblerNote:
**[Cuxton to Sole Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cuxton-to-sole-street/)**: Open downland views, wooded ridge, bluebell woods, organic vineyard, scenic ridges, and village churchyards. Sights: Cobham Church, Leather Bottle pub. Some strenuous climbs. Best in late April/May. Lunch at the Golden Lion in Luddesdown, or the Leather Bottle in Cobham. 17 km.

**[Farningham Road to Sole Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-sole-street/)**: Urban sprawl, housing developments, busy roads, arable fields, and occasional glimpses of historic rural Kent. Sights: Wheatsheaf pub, Chapter House Farm. Lunch at the [Ship Inn](https://www.shipatsouthfleet.co.uk/) in Southfleet, the [Manor Farm Barn](https://www.manorfarmbarnsouthfleet.co.uk/) in Southfleet, or the [Railway Inn](http://www.railwayinnsolestreet.co.uk/) in Sole Street. 17 km.

**Sole Street circular**: a shorter variant of the [Meopham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/meopham-to-rochester/) walk. Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, community woodland, landscaped parkland, wildflower meadows, farm fields, and country park. Sights: [Jeskyns Community Woodland](https://www.forestryengland.uk/jeskyns), [Ashenbank Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/ashenbank-wood), [Cobham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cobham,_Kent), [Cobham Park (Cobham Wood and Mausoleum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/cobham-wood-and-mausoleum), Darnley Mausoleum, [Ranscombe Farm Nature Reserve](https://www.plantlife.org.uk/our-work/ranscombe-farm-nature-reserve), St Mary Magdalene Church, New College of Cobham, Bronze Age Barrow, Cobham Hall, Luddesdown Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leather Bottle](https://www.theleatherbottle.pub/index) in Cobham, the Darnley Arms in Cobham, or the [Ship Inn](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/kent/ship-inn) in Cobham. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Snodland to Sole Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/snodland-to-sole-street/)**: North Downs, wooded areas, open fields, hilly sections, pastoral valleys, organic farmland. Sights: Snodland Clock Tower, Hall House, Luddesdown Court, St Peter and Paul church. Nettles in summer. 13 km. 4 hours.

0.3842646,51.6670001.name: Ingatestone

Sights: Hadleigh Castle, Two Tree Island, Benfleet Creek. Can be muddy. Best May to October. Lunch at the Crooked Billet in Leigh-on-Sea, or the Osborne Bros in Leigh-on-Sea. 15 km.

Sights: [Eastbourne Pier](http://www.eastbournepier.com/), [Eastbourne Harbour](http://www.eastbourneharbour.com/walks.php), [Martello Towers](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martello_tower), [Pevensey Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/daysout/properties/pevensey-castle/), [De La Warr Pavilion](http://www.dlwp.com/), Hastings Pier. Shingle heavy going. Lunch at the Aqua Bar in Pevensey Bay, the Moorings in Pevensey Bay, the [Castle Inn](http://castleinnpevenseybay.co.uk/) in Pevensey Bay, the [Star Inn](https://www.thestarinnnormansbay.co.uk/) in Norman's Bay, the [Relais Cooden Beach](https://www.therelaisretreats.com/coodenbeach/) in Cooden Beach, the [De La Warr Pavilion Cafe, Bar & Kitchen](http://www.dlwp.com/) in Bexhill, the [Farmyard](https://farmyardwine.com/) in St Leonard's, or the [Royal](http://theroyalstleonards.co.uk/) in St Leonard's. 26 km.

**[Hastings Circular via Fairlight Glen Beach](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hastings-circular-via-fairlight-glen-beach/)**: Hilly clifftop path, steep glens, shingle and sandy beach, forested inland valleys, coastal headlands, bracken, and eroding cliffs. Sights: Hastings Castle, Jerwood Art Gallery, Fairlight Glen Beach, [St Andrew's Church](http://www.sussexparishchurches.org/content/view/170/34/), [Hastings County Park](http://www.1066online.com/HASTINGS-MAIN/Places-to-Visit/visit-hastings/country-park.html). Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the [Coastguards Tea Rooms](http://www.coastguardstearooms.co.uk/) in Fairlight. 13 km.

**[Hastings to Rye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hastings-to-rye/)**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, remote naturist beach, flat marshland, ancient hilltop towns, panoramic viewpoints. Sights: Hastings Castle, St Clements Caves, New Gate, St. Thomas' Church, The Look Out, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php). Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the [Cove](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk) in Fairlight, the [Coastguard Café](https://www.coastguardcafe.co.uk/) in Pett Level, or the [Eater's](https://www.facebook.com/eatersatpett/) in Pett Level. 19 km. 6 hours.

**[Winchelsea to Hastings](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings/)**: Rambler favourite! Flat river levels, farmland, hilly coastal cliffs, wooded glens, naturist beach, historic old town. Sights: All Saints Church, Hog Hill windmill, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php), Fairlight Glen nudist beach, Hastings Castle. Steep cliff climbs. Best in summer or spring. Lunch at the Queens Head in Icklesham, the Royal Oak Inn in Pett, the [C-Side cafe](https://www.csidecafe.co.uk/) in Pett Level, the [Cockles and Dreams](https://www.facebook.com/cocklesanddreams/) in Pett Level, or the [Cove Inn](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk/) in Fairlight Cove. 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/)**: Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, hilly territory with fine views, coastal cliff path with steep ascents and descents, historic walled town. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, Icklesham Church, Doleham station, Fairlight Hall, Fairlight Glen, Ecclesbourne Glen, East Hill Lift, Hastings Old Town. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the Three Oaks pub in Three Oaks, the Coastguard Cafe in Fairlight, or the Grumpy Cook Cafe in Fairlight. 26 km.

Hilly woodland, narrow grassy paths, Hadleigh Country Park, estuary views. Sights: Hadleigh Castle. Can be muddy. Best May to October. Lunch at the Tides cafe in Chalkwell, the Beach Hut in Westcliff, the Oliver's cafe in Southend-on-Sea, the Billy Hundreds in Southchurch, the Ocean Beach in Southchurch, or the Uncle Tom's Cabin in Shoebury Common. 12 km.

Flat Medway Valley start, gentle Greensand Ridge climb, arable fields, apple orchards, scenic ridge walking, coppiced woodland with wood anemone, then descent to Harrietsham. Sights: Boughton Monchelsea church, Buttercup Goat Sanctuary, Pepper Box pub. Late March or early April. Lunch at the Bull in Linton. 24 km.

**[Harrietsham or Sutton Valence to Pluckley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harrietsham-to-pluckley/)**: Gentle ridge, apple orchards, pasture, escarpment views, bluebell woods, Kentish farmland, churches attached to farms and manor houses. **[North Fambridge to Burnham-on-Crouch](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/north-fambridge-to-burnham-on-crouch/)**: Flat sea wall path, tidal river, salt marshes, mudflats, reed ponds, creeks, marinas, and riverside promenade. Lunch at the Three Horseshoes in Althorne. 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**[South Woodham Ferrers to North Fambridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/south-woodham-ferrers-to-north-fambridge/)**: Rolling hills, arable fields, vineyards, bluebell woods, disused railway line, tidal riverside, grassy salt marshes. Sights: RHS Garden Hyde Hall, Blue House Farm Nature Reserve, Martins Lane Estate, Crows Lane Estate. Can be muddy. Best in bluebell season. Lunch at the [Vita Bella](https://www.vita-bella.co.uk/) in East Hanningfield. 25 km. 5.5 hours.

0.7025193,50.9339128.name: Winchelsea
0.7025193,50.9339128.privateNote: Trainline says 1h12m — St Pancras via Ashford International
0.7025193,50.9339128.ramblerNote:
**Hastings to Winchelsea**: a shorter variant of the [Hastings to Rye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hastings-to-rye/) walk. Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, naturist beach, flat marshland, ancient hilltop town. Sights: Hastings Castle, St Clements Caves, New Gate, St. Thomas' Church. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the [Cove](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk) in Fairlight. 16 km.

**[Winchelsea to Hastings](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings/)**: Rambler favourite! Flat river levels, farmland, hilly coastal cliffs, wooded glens, naturist beach, historic old town. Sights: All Saints Church, Hog Hill windmill, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php), Fairlight Glen nudist beach, Hastings Castle. Steep cliff climbs. Best in summer or spring. Lunch at the Queens Head in Icklesham, the Royal Oak Inn in Pett, the [C-Side cafe](https://www.csidecafe.co.uk/) in Pett Level, the [Cockles and Dreams](https://www.facebook.com/cocklesanddreams/) in Pett Level, or the [Cove Inn](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk/) in Fairlight Cove. 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/)**: Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, hilly territory with fine views, coastal cliff path with steep ascents and descents, historic walled town. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, Icklesham Church, Doleham station, Fairlight Hall, Fairlight Glen, Ecclesbourne Glen, East Hill Lift, Hastings Old Town. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the Three Oaks pub in Three Oaks, the Coastguard Cafe in Fairlight, or the Grumpy Cook Cafe in Fairlight. 26 km.

0.7026932,51.8409896.name: Kelvedon
0.7026932,51.8409896.ramblerNote: **[Kelvedon Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kelvedon-circular/)**: Gentle valley, river stretches, country lanes, green lanes, field boundaries, rolling Essex countryside, fields, woods. Sights: Grange Barn, Paycocke's, Marks Hall Gardens and Arboretum, All Saints Church, Coggeshall Abbey. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [West Street Vineyard](http://www.weststreetvineyard.co.uk/) in Coggeshall, the [Orchard Kitchen Café](https://www.markshall.org.uk/plan-your-visit/cafe/) in Marks Hall, the [Ranfield's Brasserie](https://ranfieldsbrasserie.co.uk/) in Coggeshall, the Bakes and Blooms in Coggeshall, the [Chapel Inn](http://www.thechapelinn.com) in Coggeshall, the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.greenekinginns.co.uk/hotels/white-hart-coggeshall/) in Coggeshall, or the Woolpack Inn in Coggeshall. 23 km. 5 hours.

Lunch at the [Harrow Hill Hotel & Inn](http://www.harrowhillhotel.com/) in Warren Street, Lenham, or the [Plough](http://www.theploughinnstalisfield.co.uk/) in Stalisfield Green. 13 km. 4.5 hours.
