0.7310208,50.952632.ramblerNote:
**[Hastings to Rye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hastings-to-rye/)**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, remote naturist beach, flat marshland, ancient hilltop towns, panoramic viewpoints. Sights: Hastings Castle, St Clements Caves, New Gate, St. Thomas' Church, The Look Out, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php). Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the [Cove](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk) in Fairlight, the [Coastguard Café](https://www.coastguardcafe.co.uk/) in Pett Level, or the [Eater's](https://www.facebook.com/eatersatpett/) in Pett Level. 19 km. 6 hours.

**[Rye Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rye-figure-of-8/)**: Medieval hilltop town, reclaimed marshland, shingle beach, saline lagoons, freshwater gravel pits, reedbeds, riverside embankments, and coastal nature reserve. Sights: [St Mary the Virgin](https://www.ryeparishchurch.org.uk/history.htm), [Rye Castle Museum](http://ryemuseum.co.uk), [Lamb House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/lamb-house), [Rye Heritage Centre](https://www.ryeheritage.co.uk), [Rye Harbour Nature Reserve](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk), Camber Castle, Castle Water, Mary Stanford Lifeboat House, Ypres Tower, Landgate, Mermaid Inn, Rye Windmill, Saltcote Place, Church of the Holy Spirit, Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [William the Conqueror](https://www.williamtheconqueror.co.uk) in Rye Harbour, the Bosun's Bite in Rye Harbour, or the [Lime Kiln Cafe](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/discovery-centre/cafe) in Rye Harbour. 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Rye to Hastings**: a longer variant of the [Winchelsea to Hastings](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings/) walk. Rambler favourite! Flat levels, farmland, hilly coastal cliffs, wooded glens, naturist beach, historic old town. Sights: All Saints Church, Hog Hill windmill, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php), Fairlight Glen nudist beach, Hastings Castle. Steep cliff climbs. Best in summer or spring. Lunch at the Queens Head in Icklesham, the Royal Oak Inn in Pett, the [C-Side cafe](https://www.csidecafe.co.uk/) in Pett Level, the [Cockles and Dreams](https://www.facebook.com/cocklesanddreams/) in Pett Level, or the [Cove Inn](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk/) in Fairlight Cove. 24 km.

**Rye circular**: a shorter variant of the [Rye, Dungeness and Lydd-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rye-dungeness-lydd-on-sea/) walk. River estuary, sand dunes, beach, grassy marshland. Sights: Camber Sands, Romney Marsh Windfarm. Check tide timings. Lunch at the [Owl](http://theowlcamber.co.uk/) in Camber, the [Gallivant](https://thegallivant.co.uk/) in Camber, the Rye Bay Café/Bistro in Camber, or the Dunes Bar & Restaurant in Camber. 17 km.

**[Harrietsham or Sutton Valence to Pluckley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harrietsham-to-pluckley/)**: Gentle ridge, apple orchards, pasture, escarpment views, bluebell woods, Kentish farmland, churches attached to farms and manor houses. Can be muddy. Late April or May. Lunch at the Pepper Box Inn in Fairbourne Heath. 15 km.

**[Pluckley Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pluckley-circular/)**: Flat Low Weald farmland, orchards, quiet lanes, ancient pubs, quaint Kent villages, and a fine viewpoint near Pluckley. Sights: Dering windows, St Nicholas Church, St Mary the Virgin, Pluckley Station, H E Bates' home in Little Chart Forstal. Best late April/early May. Lunch at the Swan Inn in Little Chart, the Black Horse in Pluckley, or the Dering Arms in Pluckley. 11 km.

**[Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/)**: North Downs escarpment ridge, open downland, woods, sloping fields, fine views over Kent coast. Sights: Tickled Trout. **[Wye Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wye-circular/)**: North Downs escarpments, chalk downland, beech and chestnut woods, open fields, riverbank along Great Stour, churchyard, farmland. Lunch at the Timber Batts at the Compasses in Sole Street, Crundale. 17 km. 5 hours.

Sights: Nottage Maritime Institute, St Mary's Church, River Colne Flood Surge Barrier, Church of St Lawrence, Church of St Andrew, Fingringhoe Mill, John Brunning Walk. Check ferry timetable. Lunch at the Rose & Crown in Wivenhoe, the Greyhound in Wivenhoe, the Anchor in Rowhedge, or the Ye Olde Albion in Rowhedge. 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**Wivenhoe to Clacton-on-Sea**: a longer variant of the [Wivenhoe to West Mersea or Clacton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivenhoe-to-west-mersea-or-clacton-on-sea/) walk. Tidal river estuary, saltings, mud flats, disused railway trackbed, harbourfront, ferry crossing, coastal sea wall, caravan park frontages, sandy beach, seaside town promenade. Sights: Jaywick Martello Tower, [Colne Point National Nature Reserve](http://www.essexwt.org.uk/reserves/colne-point). Check ferry times. Lunch at the No. 1 Harbour Square in Brightlingsea, the [Yachtsman's Arms](https://www.facebook.com/theyachtsmansarms) in Brightlingsea, or the Ferry Boat Inn in Point Clear.

Sights: Godmersham Park, [Chilham Castle](http://www.chilham-castle.co.uk). Best in late Apr/May. Lunch at the Timber Batts @ The Compasses in Sole Street. 15 km.

**[Chilham to Canterbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chilham-to-canterbury/)**: River valleys, lakes, hop fields, apple orchards, mixed woodland, heathland, nature reserve, university parkland, medieval city streets. 1.3986272,52.1510336.ramblerNote: **[Wickham Market to Felixstowe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickham-market-to-felixstowe/)**: Farmland, Suffolk heathland (Sandlings), river bank, grassy sea wall, shingle beach, isolated coastline, and urban Felixstowe approaches. Sights: Sandlings Walk, Suffolk Coast Path, Havergate Island RSPB Reserve, Shingle Street, Bawdsey Manor, Orford Ness, Martello Towers, Hollesley Marshes Nature Reserve, Butley Ferry. Long shingle sections. Lunch at the [Green Man](http://www.greenmantunstall.co.uk/) in Tunstall, or the [Froize Freehouse](http://froize.co.uk/) in Chillesford. 35 km.

1.3986457,51.2231068.name: Deal
1.3986457,51.2231068.ramblerNote: **[Dover to Deal](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dover-to-deal/)**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop chalk paths, two significant climbs, level pebble beach coastpath, open sea views, historic town. Sights: Dover Castle, South Foreland Lighthouse, [Walmer Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), [Deal Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/). Mobile may switch networks. Late May/June for flora. Lunch at the White Cliffs Visitor Centre in Dover, the Mrs Knott's Tea Room in South Foreland Lighthouse, the Pines Garden Tea Room and Museum in St Margaret's Bay, the Coastguard in St Margaret's Bay, the Zetland Arms in Kingsdown, the Deal Pier Kitchen in Deal, the Port Arms in Deal, the Kings Arm in Deal, or the Ship Inn in Deal. 17 km.

1.4052264,51.3410764.name: Ramsgate
1.4052264,51.3410764.ramblerNote: **[Ramsgate to Margate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ramsgate-to-margate/)**: Rambler favourite! Low chalk cliffs, sandy bays and coves, cliff-top grassland, seaside promenades, and historic coastal towns. Sights: Turner Contemporary Gallery, Dickens House Museum, Kingsgate Castle, North Foreland Lighthouse, [Royal Victoria Pavilion](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/the-royal-victoria-pavilion-ramsgate). Check tide timings. Lunch at the [Royal Victoria Pavillion](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/the-royal-victoria-pavilion-ramsgate) in Ramsgate, the [Royal Harbour Brasserie](http://www.royalharbourbrasserie.co.uk/) in Ramsgate, the [Sams](https://twitter.com/dumptoncafe) in Dumpton Gap, the [Louisa Bay Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/Louisa-Bay-Cafe-260058987367161/) in Louisa Bay, the Charles Dickens in Broadstairs, the [Captain Digby](http://www.captaindigby.co.uk/) in Kingsgate Bay, or the [Botany Bay](https://www.botanybayhotel.co.uk/) in Botany Bay. 15 km.

1.4334122,51.360737.name: Broadstairs
1.4334122,51.360737.ramblerNote: **Broadstairs circular**: a variant of the [Ramsgate to Margate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ramsgate-to-margate/) walk. Rambler favourite! Low chalk cliffs, sandy bays and coves, cliff-top grassland, seaside promenades. Sights: Dickens House Museum, Turner Contemporary Gallery, North Foreland Lighthouse. Check tide timings. Lunch at the Charles Dickens in Broadstairs, the [Captain Digby](http://www.captaindigby.co.uk/) in Kingsgate Bay, or the [Botany Bay](https://www.botanybayhotel.co.uk/) in Botany Bay.
